            "line": 907,
            "column": 72
          }
        },
        "contents": "    }\n\n    fn binary(\n        &amp;self,\n        func: &amp;BinaryFunc,\n        left: Self::Summary,\n        right: Self::Summary,\n    ) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let (left_monotonic, right_monotonic) = func.is_monotone();\n        let fallible = func.could_error() || left.range.fallible || right.range.fallible;\n\n        let mapped_spec = if let Some(special) = SpecialBinary::for_func(func) {\n            (special.map_fn)(left.range, right.range)\n        } else {\n            let mut expr = MirScalarExpr::CallBinary {\n                func: func.clone(),\n                expr1: Box::new(Self::placeholder(left.col_type.clone())),\n                expr2: Box::new(Self::placeholder(right.col_type.clone())),\n            };\n            left.range.flat_map(left_monotonic, |left_result| {\n                Self::set_argument(&amp;mut expr, 0, left_result);\n                right.range.flat_map(right_monotonic, |right_result| {\n                    Self::set_argument(&amp;mut expr, 1, right_result);\n                    self.eval_result(expr.eval(&amp;[], self.arena))\n                })\n            })\n        };\n\n        let col_type = func.output_type(left.col_type, right.col_type);\n\n        let range = mapped_spec.intersect(ResultSpec::has_type(&amp;col_type, fallible));\n        ColumnSpec { col_type, range }\n    }\n\n    fn variadic(&amp;self, func: &amp;VariadicFunc, args: Vec&lt;Self::Summary&gt;) -&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

            "lineNumber": 891
          },
          {
            "text": "            };",
            "lineNumber": 892
          },

            "text": "            left.range.flat_map(left_monotonic, |left_result| {",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/expr/src/scalar.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 3135,
            "column": 5
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 3166,
            "column": 79
          }
        },
        "contents": "    fn from_proto(proto: ProtoEvalError) -&gt; Result&lt;Self, TryFromProtoError&gt; {\n        use proto_eval_error::Kind::*;\n        match proto.kind {\n            Some(kind) =&gt; match kind {\n                CharacterNotValidForEncoding(v) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::CharacterNotValidForEncoding(v)),\n                CharacterTooLargeForEncoding(v) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::CharacterTooLargeForEncoding(v)),\n                DateBinOutOfRange(v) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::DateBinOutOfRange(v.into())),\n                DivisionByZero(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::DivisionByZero),\n                Unsupported(v) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Unsupported {\n                    feature: v.feature.into(),\n                    discussion_no: v.discussion_no.into_rust()?,\n                }),\n                FloatOverflow(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::FloatOverflow),\n                FloatUnderflow(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::FloatUnderflow),\n                NumericFieldOverflow(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::NumericFieldOverflow),\n                Float32OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Float32OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Float64OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Float64OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Int16OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Int16OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Int32OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Int32OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Int64OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::Int64OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Uint16OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::UInt16OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Uint32OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::UInt32OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                Uint64OutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::UInt64OutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                MzTimestampOutOfRange(val) =&gt; {\n                    Ok(EvalError::MzTimestampOutOfRange(val.value.into()))\n                }\n                MzTimestampStepOverflow(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::MzTimestampStepOverflow),\n                OidOutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::OidOutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                IntervalOutOfRange(val) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::IntervalOutOfRange(val.value.into())),\n                TimestampCannotBeNan(()) =&gt; Ok(EvalError::TimestampCannotBeNan)",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl EvalError {\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            EvalError::IncompatibleArrayDimensions { dims: None } =&gt; Some(\n                \"Arrays with differing dimensions are not compatible for concatenation.\".into(),\n            ),\n            EvalError::IncompatibleArrayDimensions {\n                dims: Some((a_dims, b_dims)),\n            } =&gt; Some(format!(\n                \"Arrays of {} and {} dimensions are not compatible for concatenation.\",\n                a_dims, b_dims\n            )),\n            EvalError::InvalidIdentifier { detail, .. } =&gt; detail.as_deref().map(Into::into),\n            EvalError::ArrayFillWrongArraySubscripts =&gt; {\n                Some(\"Low bound array has different size than dimensions array.\".into())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn hint(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            EvalError::InvalidBase64EndSequence =&gt; Some(\n                \"Input data is missing padding, is truncated, or is otherwise corrupted.\".into(),\n            ),\n            EvalError::LikeEscapeTooLong =&gt; {\n                Some(\"Escape string must be empty or one character.\".into())\n            }\n            EvalError::MzTimestampOutOfRange(_) =&gt; Some(\n                \"Integer, numeric, and text casts to mz_timestamp must be in the form of whole \\\n                milliseconds since the Unix epoch. Values with fractional parts cannot be \\\n                converted to mz_timestamp.\"\n                    .into(),\n            ),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::error::Error for EvalError {}\n\nimpl From&lt;ParseError&gt; for EvalError {\n    fn from(e: ParseError) -&gt; EvalError {\n        EvalError::Parse(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;ParseHexError&gt; for EvalError {\n    fn from(e: ParseHexError) -&gt; EvalError {\n        EvalError::ParseHex(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;InvalidArrayError&gt; for EvalError {\n    fn from(e: InvalidArrayError) -&gt; EvalError {\n        EvalError::InvalidArray(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;RegexCompilationError&gt; for EvalError",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/expr/src/interpret.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 481,
            "column": 5
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 536
          }
        },
        "contents": "}\n\nimpl&lt;'a, E: Interpreter + ?Sized&gt; Interpreter for MfpEval&lt;'a, E&gt; {\n    type Summary = E::Summary;\n\n    fn column(&amp;self, id: usize) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        if id &lt; self.input_arity {\n            self.evaluator.column(id)\n        } else {\n            self.expressions[id - self.input_arity].clone()\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn literal(&amp;self, result: &amp;Result&lt;Row, EvalError&gt;, col_type: &amp;SqlColumnType) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.literal(result, col_type)\n    }\n\n    fn unmaterializable(&amp;self, func: &amp;UnmaterializableFunc) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.unmaterializable(func)\n    }\n\n    fn unary(&amp;self, func: &amp;UnaryFunc, expr: Self::Summary) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.unary(func, expr)\n    }\n\n    fn binary(\n        &amp;self,\n        func: &amp;BinaryFunc,\n        left: Self::Summary,\n        right: Self::Summary,\n    ) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.binary(func, left, right)\n    }\n\n    fn variadic(&amp;self, func: &amp;VariadicFunc, exprs: Vec&lt;Self::Summary&gt;) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.variadic(func, exprs)\n    }\n\n    fn cond(&amp;self, cond: Self::Summary, then: Self::Summary, els: Self::Summary) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        self.evaluator.cond(cond, then, els)\n    }\n}\n\n/// A unary function we've added special-case handling for; including:\n/// - A three-argument function, taking and returning [ResultSpec]s. This\n///   overrides the default function-handling logic entirely.\n/// - Metadata on whether / not this function is pushdownable. See [Trace].\nstruct SpecialUnary {\n    map_fn: for&lt;'a, 'b&gt; fn(&amp;'b ColumnSpecs&lt;'a&gt;, ResultSpec&lt;'a&gt;) -&gt; ResultSpec&lt;'a&gt;,\n    pushdownable: bool,\n}\n\nimpl SpecialUnary {\n    /// Returns the special-case handling for a particular function, if it exists.",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/expr/src/linear.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 308,
            "column": 47
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 348
          }
        },
        "contents": "(\n        expr: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n    ) -&gt; (Self, &amp;mut MirRelationExpr) {\n        // This is essentially the same code as &#96;extract_non_errors_from_expr&#96;, except the seemingly\n        // superfluous outer if, which works around a borrow-checker issue:\n        // https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/54663\n        if matches!(expr, MirRelationExpr::Map { input: _, scalars } if scalars.iter().all(|s| !s.is_literal_err()))\n            || matches!(expr, MirRelationExpr::Filter { input: _, predicates } if predicates.iter().all(|p| !p.is_literal_err()))\n            || matches!(expr, MirRelationExpr::Project { .. })\n        {\n            match expr {\n                MirRelationExpr::Map { input, scalars }\n                    if scalars.iter().all(|s| !s.is_literal_err()) =&gt;\n                {\n                    let (mfp, expr) = Self::extract_non_errors_from_expr_ref_mut(input);\n                    (mfp.map(scalars.iter().cloned()), expr)\n                }\n                MirRelationExpr::Filter { input, predicates }\n                    if predicates.iter().all(|p| !p.is_literal_err()) =&gt;\n                {\n                    let (mfp, expr) = Self::extract_non_errors_from_expr_ref_mut(input);\n                    (mfp.filter(predicates.iter().cloned()), expr)\n                }\n                MirRelationExpr::Project { input, outputs } =&gt; {\n                    let (mfp, expr) = Self::extract_non_errors_from_expr_ref_mut(input);\n                    (mfp.project(outputs.iter().cloned()), expr)\n                }\n                _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n            }\n        } else {\n            (Self::new(expr.arity()), expr)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Removes an error-free MapFilterProject from the root of the expression.\n    ///\n    /// The expression will be modified to extract maps, filters, and projects\n    /// from the root of the expression, which will be returned as &#96;Self&#96;. The\n    /// extraction will halt if a Map or Filter containing a literal error is\n    /// reached. Otherwise, the method will return an",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "(",
            "lineNumber": 309
          },
          {
            "text": "        expr: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,",
            "lineNumber": 310
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; (Self, &amp;mut MirRelationExpr) {",
            "lineNumber": 311
          },
          {
            "text": "        // This is essentially the same code as &#96;extract_non_errors_from_expr&#96;, except the seemingly",
            "lineNumber": 312
          },
          {
            "text": "        // superfluous outer if, which works around a borrow-checker issue:",
            "lineNumber": 313
          },
          {
            "text": "        // https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/54663",
            "lineNumber": 314
          },
          {
            "text": "        if matches!(expr, MirRelationExpr::Map { input: _, scalars } if scalars.iter().all(|s| !s.is_literal_err()))",
            "lineNumber": 315
          },

          }
        },
        "contents": "impl&lt;'a&gt; Interpreter for ColumnSpecs&lt;'a&gt; {\n    type Summary = ColumnSpec&lt;'a&gt;;\n\n    fn column(&amp;self, id: usize) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let col_type = self.relation.column_types[id].clone();\n        let range = self.columns[id].clone();\n        ColumnSpec { col_type, range }\n    }\n\n    fn literal(&amp;self, result: &amp;Result&lt;Row, EvalError&gt;, col_type: &amp;SqlColumnType) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let col_type = col_type.clone();\n        let range = self.eval_result(result.as_ref().map(|row| {\n            self.arena\n                .make_datum(|packer| packer.push(row.unpack_first()))\n        }));\n        ColumnSpec { col_type, range }\n    }\n\n    fn unmaterializable(&amp;self, func: &amp;UnmaterializableFunc) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let col_type = func.output_type();\n        let range = self\n            .unmaterializables\n            .get(func)\n            .cloned()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| ResultSpec::has_type(&amp;func.output_type(), true));\n        ColumnSpec { col_type, range }\n    }\n\n    fn unary(&amp;self, func: &amp;UnaryFunc, summary: Self::Summary) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let fallible = func.could_error() || summary.range.fallible;\n        let mapped_spec = if let Some(special) = SpecialUnary::for_func(func) {\n            (special.map_fn)(self, summary.range)\n        } else {\n            let is_monotone = func.is_monotone();\n            let mut expr = MirScalarExpr::CallUnary {\n                func: func.clone(),\n                expr: Box::new(Self::placeholder(summary.col_type.clone())),\n            };\n            summary.range.flat_map(is_monotone, |datum| {\n                Self::set_argument(&amp;mut expr, 0, datum);\n                self.eval_result(expr.eval(&amp;[], self.arena))\n            })\n        };\n\n        let col_type = func.output_type(summary.col_type);\n\n        let range = mapped_spec.intersect(ResultSpec::has_type(&amp;col_type, fallible));\n        ColumnSpec { col_type, range }\n    }\n\n    fn binary(\n        &amp;self,\n        func: &amp;BinaryFunc,",

            "column": 16
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl&lt;T: for&lt;'a&gt; EagerUnaryFunc&lt;'a&gt;&gt; LazyUnaryFunc for T {\n    fn eval&lt;'a&gt;(\n        &amp;'a self,\n        datums: &amp;[Datum&lt;'a&gt;],\n        temp_storage: &amp;'a RowArena,\n        a: &amp;'a MirScalarExpr,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;Datum&lt;'a&gt;, EvalError&gt; {\n        match T::Input::try_from_result(a.eval(datums, temp_storage)) {\n            // If we can convert to the input type then we call the function\n            Ok(input) =&gt; self.call(input).into_result(temp_storage),\n            // If we can't and we got a non-null datum something went wrong in the planner\n            Err(Ok(datum)) if !datum.is_null() =&gt; {\n                Err(EvalError::Internal(\"invalid input type\".into()))\n            }\n            // Otherwise we just propagate NULLs and errors\n            Err(res) =&gt; res,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn output_type(&amp;self, input_type: SqlColumnType) -&gt; SqlColumnType {\n        self.output_type(input_type)\n    }\n\n    fn propagates_nulls(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        self.propagates_nulls()\n    }\n\n    fn introduces_nulls(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        self.introduces_nulls()\n    }\n\n    fn could_error(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        self.could_error()\n    }\n\n    fn preserves_uniqueness(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        self.preserves_uniqueness()\n    }\n\n    fn inverse(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;crate::UnaryFunc&gt; {\n        self.inverse()\n    }\n\n    fn is_monotone(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        self.is_monotone()\n    }\n}\n\nderive_unary!(\n    Not,\n    IsNull,\n    IsTrue,\n    IsFalse,\n    BitNotInt16,",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/expr/src/interpret.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 437,
            "column": 48
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 484,
            "column": 64
          }
        },
        "contents": " Self::Summary {\n        let mfp_eval = MfpEval::new(self, plan.mfp.input_arity, &amp;plan.mfp.expressions);\n        // NB: self should not be used after this point!\n        let mut results: Vec&lt;_&gt; = plan\n            .mfp\n            .predicates\n            .iter()\n            .map(|(_, e)| mfp_eval.expr(e))\n            .collect();\n        let mz_now = mfp_eval.unmaterializable(&amp;UnmaterializableFunc::MzNow);\n        for bound in &amp;plan.lower_bounds {\n            let bound_range = mfp_eval.expr(bound);\n            let result = mfp_eval.binary(&amp;BinaryFunc::Lte(func::Lte), bound_range, mz_now.clone());\n            results.push(result);\n        }\n        for bound in &amp;plan.upper_bounds {\n            let bound_range = mfp_eval.expr(bound);\n            let result = mfp_eval.binary(&amp;BinaryFunc::Gte(func::Gte), bound_range, mz_now.clone());\n            results.push(result);\n        }\n        self.variadic(&amp;VariadicFunc::And, results)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wrap another interpreter, but tack a few extra columns on at the end. An internal implementation\n/// detail of &#96;eval_mfp&#96; and &#96;eval_mfp_plan&#96;.\npub(crate) struct MfpEval&lt;'a, E: Interpreter + ?Sized&gt; {\n    evaluator: &amp;'a E,\n    input_arity: usize,\n    expressions: Vec&lt;E::Summary&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a, E: Interpreter + ?Sized&gt; MfpEval&lt;'a, E&gt; {\n    pub(crate) fn new(evaluator: &amp;'a E, input_arity: usize, expressions: &amp;[MirScalarExpr]) -&gt; Self {\n        let mut mfp_eval = MfpEval {\n            evaluator,\n            input_arity,\n            expressions: vec![],\n        };\n        for expr in expressions {\n            let result = mfp_eval.expr(expr);\n            mfp_eval.expressions.push(result);\n        }\n        mfp_eval\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a, E: Interpreter + ?Sized&gt; Interpreter for MfpEval&lt;'a, E&gt;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "}",

          {
            "lineNumber": 461
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Wrap another interpreter, but tack a few extra columns on at the end. An internal implementation",

          {
            "text": "/// detail of &#96;eval_mfp&#96; and &#96;eval_mfp_plan&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 463
          },
          {
            "text": "pub(crate) struct MfpEval&lt;'a, E: Interpreter + ?Sized&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 464

        "contents": "                            if exprs.len() == 1 {\n                                // Only one argument, so the coalesce is a no-op.\n                                *e = exprs[0].take();\n                            }\n                        } else if exprs.iter().all(|e| e.is_literal()) {\n                            *e = eval(e);\n                        } else if func.propagates_nulls()\n                            &amp;&amp; exprs.iter().any(|e| e.is_literal_null())\n                        {\n                            *e = MirScalarExpr::literal_null(e.typ(column_types).scalar_type);\n                        } else if let Some(err) = exprs.iter().find_map(|e| e.as_literal_err()) {\n                            *e = MirScalarExpr::literal(\n                                Err(err.clone()),\n                                e.typ(column_types).scalar_type,\n                            );\n                        } else if *func == VariadicFunc::RegexpMatch\n                            &amp;&amp; exprs[1].is_literal()\n                            &amp;&amp;",

        "contents": "impl Interpreter for Trace {\n    type Summary = TraceSummary;\n\n    fn column(&amp;self, _id: usize) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        TraceSummary::Dynamic\n    }\n\n    fn literal(\n        &amp;self,\n        _result: &amp;Result&lt;Row, EvalError&gt;,\n        _col_type: &amp;SqlColumnType,\n    ) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        TraceSummary::Constant\n    }\n\n    fn unmaterializable(&amp;self, _func: &amp;UnmaterializableFunc) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        TraceSummary::Dynamic\n    }\n\n    fn unary(&amp;self, func: &amp;UnaryFunc, expr: Self::Summary) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let pushdownable = match SpecialUnary::for_func(func) {\n            None =&gt; func.is_monotone(),\n            Some(special) =&gt; special.pushdownable,\n        };\n        expr.apply_fn(pushdownable)\n    }\n\n    fn binary(\n        &amp;self,\n        func: &amp;BinaryFunc,\n        left: Self::Summary,\n        right: Self::Summary,\n    ) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        let (left_pushdownable, right_pushdownable) = match SpecialBinary::for_func(func) {\n            None =&gt; func.is_monotone(),\n            Some(special) =&gt; special.pushdownable,\n        };\n        left.apply_fn(left_pushdownable)\n            .max(right.apply_fn(right_pushdownable))\n    }\n\n    fn variadic(&amp;self, func: &amp;VariadicFunc, exprs: Vec&lt;Self::Summary&gt;) -&gt; Self::Summary {\n        if !func.is_associative() &amp;&amp; exprs.len() &gt;= ColumnSpecs::MAX_EVAL_ARGS {\n            // We can't efficiently evaluate functions with very large argument lists;\n            // see the comment on ColumnSpecs::MAX_EVAL_ARGS for details.\n            return TraceSummary::Unknown;\n        }\n\n        let pushdownable_fn = func.is_monotone();\n        exprs\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|pushdownable_arg| pushdownable_arg.apply_fn(pushdownable_fn))\n            .max()\n            .unwrap_or(TraceSummary::Constant)\n    }\n\n    fn cond(&amp;self, cond: Self::Summary, then: Self::Summary,",

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 348
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl MaybeShouldPanic for TransformError {\n    fn should_panic(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            TransformError::CallerShouldPanic(msg) =&gt; Some(msg.to_string()),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Catch panics in the given optimization, and demote them to [&#96;TransformError::Internal&#96;] error.\n///\n/// Additionally, if the result of the optimization is an error (not a panic) that indicates we\n/// should panic, then panic.\npub fn catch_unwind_optimize&lt;Opt, To, E&gt;(optimization: Opt) -&gt; Result&lt;To, E&gt;\nwhere\n    Opt: FnOnce() -&gt; Result&lt;To, E&gt;,\n    E: From&lt;TransformError&gt; + MaybeShouldPanic,\n{\n    match mz_ore::panic::catch_unwind_str(AssertUnwindSafe(optimization)) {\n        Ok(Err(e)) if e.should_panic().is_some() =&gt; {\n            // Promote a &#96;CallerShouldPanic&#96; error from the result to a proper panic. This is\n            // needed in order to ensure that &#96;mz_unsafe.mz_panic('forced panic')&#96; calls still\n            // panic the caller.\n            panic!(\"{}\", e.should_panic().expect(\"checked above\"));\n        }\n        Ok(result) =&gt; result.map_err(|e| e),\n        Err(panic) =&gt; {\n            // A panic during optimization is always a bug; log an error so we learn about it.\n            // TODO(teskje): collect and log a backtrace from the panic site\n            tracing::error!(\"caught a panic during query optimization: {panic}\");\n\n            let msg = format!(\"unexpected panic during query optimization: {panic}\");\n            Err(TransformError::Internal(msg).into())\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A trait for a type that can answer questions about what indexes exist.\npub trait IndexOracle: fmt::Debug {\n    /// Returns an iterator over the indexes that exist on the identified",

        "contents": "#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum OptimizerError {\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    PlanError(#[from] PlanError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    RecursionLimitError(#[from] RecursionLimitError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    TransformError(#[from] TransformError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    EvalError(#[from] EvalError),\n    #[error(\"cannot materialize call to {0}\")]\n    UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc),\n    #[error(\"cannot call {func} in {context} \")]\n    UncallableFunction {\n        func: UnmaterializableFunc,\n        context: &amp;'static str,\n    },\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    UnsupportedTemporalExpression(String),\n    /// This is a specific kind of internal error. It's distinct from &#96;Internal&#96;, because we want to\n    /// catch it and swallow it in some cases.\n    #[error(\"internal optimizer error: MfpPlan couldn't be converted into SafeMfpPlan\")]\n    InternalUnsafeMfpPlan(String),\n    #[error(\"internal optimizer error: {0}\")]\n    Internal(String),\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;String&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(msg: String) -&gt; Self {\n        Self::Internal(msg)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl OptimizerError {\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Self::UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp) =&gt; {\n                Some(\"See: https://materialize.com/docs/sql/functions/now_and_mz_now/\".into())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn hint(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Self::UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp) =&gt; {\n                Some(\"In temporal filters &#96;mz_now()&#96; may work instead.\".into())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TimestampError&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(value: TimestampError) -&gt; Self {\n        OptimizerError::EvalError(EvalError::from(value))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;anyhow::Error&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(value: anyhow::Error) -&gt; Self {\n        OptimizerError::Internal(value.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MaybeShouldPanic for OptimizerError",

        },
        "contents": " =&gt; SqlState::INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES,\n            AdapterError::ResultSize(_) =&gt; SqlState::OUT_OF_MEMORY,\n            AdapterError::SafeModeViolation(_) =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n            AdapterError::SubscribeOnlyTransaction =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE,\n            AdapterError::Optimizer(e) =&gt; match e {\n                OptimizerError::PlanError(PlanError::InvalidSchemaName) =&gt; {\n                    SqlState::INVALID_SCHEMA_NAME\n                }\n                OptimizerError::PlanError(PlanError::ColumnAlreadyExists { .. }) =&gt; {\n                    SqlState::DUPLICATE_COLUMN\n                }\n                OptimizerError::PlanError(PlanError::UnknownParameter(_)) =&gt; {\n                    SqlState::UNDEFINED_PARAMETER\n                }\n                OptimizerError::PlanError(PlanError::ParameterNotAllowed(_)) =&gt; {\n                    SqlState::UNDEFINED_PARAMETER\n                }\n                OptimizerError::PlanError(_) =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n                OptimizerError::RecursionLimitError(e) =&gt; {\n                    AdapterError::RecursionLimit(e.clone()).code() // Delegate to outer\n                }\n                OptimizerError::Internal(s) =&gt; {\n                    AdapterError::Internal(s.clone()).code() // Delegate to outer\n                }\n                OptimizerError::EvalError(e) =&gt; {\n                    AdapterError::Eval(e.clone()).code() // Delegate to outer\n                }\n                OptimizerError::TransformError(_) =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n                OptimizerError::UnmaterializableFunction(_) =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n                OptimizerError::UncallableFunction { .. } =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n                OptimizerError::UnsupportedTemporalExpression(_) =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n                // This should be handled by peek optimization, so it's an internal error if it\n                // reaches the user.\n                OptimizerError::InternalUnsafeMfpPlan(_) =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n            },\n            AdapterError::UnallowedOnCluster { .. } =&gt;",

        "contents": "}\n\nimpl From&lt;ExplainError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: ExplainError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e {\n            ExplainError::RecursionLimitError(e) =&gt; AdapterError::RecursionLimit(e),\n            e =&gt; AdapterError::Explain(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_sql::catalog::CatalogError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_sql::catalog::CatalogError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Catalog(mz_catalog::memory::error::Error::from(e))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;PlanError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: PlanError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e {\n            PlanError::UnknownCursor(name) =&gt; AdapterError::UnknownCursor(name),\n            _ =&gt; AdapterError::PlanError(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;OptimizerError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: OptimizerError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        use OptimizerError::*;\n        match e {\n            PlanError(e) =&gt; Self::PlanError(e),\n            RecursionLimitError(e) =&gt; Self::RecursionLimit(e),\n            EvalError(e) =&gt; Self::Eval(e),\n            InternalUnsafeMfpPlan(e) =&gt; Self::Internal(e),\n            Internal(e) =&gt; Self::Internal(e),\n            e =&gt; Self::Optimizer(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;NotNullViolation&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: NotNullViolation) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::ConstraintViolation(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;RecursionLimitError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: RecursionLimitError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::RecursionLimit(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;oneshot::error::RecvError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: oneshot::error::RecvError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Unstructured(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;StorageError&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: StorageError&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::Storage(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;compute_error::InstanceExists&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: compute_error::InstanceExists) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::Compute(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TimestampError&gt; for AdapterError",

            "line": 721,

        "contents": "                        Err(err) =&gt; {\n                            let Some(optimizer) = optimizer.left() else {\n                                // In &#96;COPY TO&#96; contexts, immediately retire the\n                                // execution with the error.\n                                return Err(err);\n                            };\n                            let ExplainContext::Plan(explain_ctx) = explain_ctx else {\n                                // In &#96;sequence_~&#96; contexts, immediately retire the\n                                // execution with the error.\n                                return Err(err);\n                            };\n\n                            if explain_ctx.broken {\n                                // In &#96;EXPLAIN BROKEN&#96; contexts, just log the error\n                                // and move to the next stage with default\n                                // parameters.\n                                tracing::error!(\"error while handling EXPLAIN statement: {}\", err);\n                                PeekStage::ExplainPlan(PeekStageExplainPlan {\n                                    validity,\n                                    optimizer,\n                                    df_meta: Default::default(),\n                                    explain_ctx,\n                                    insights_ctx: None,\n                                })\n                            } else {\n                                // In regular &#96;EXPLAIN&#96; contexts, immediately retire\n                                // the execution with the error.\n                                return Err(err);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    };\n                    Ok(Box::new(stage))\n                })\n            },\n        )))\n    }\n\n    #[instrument]\n    async fn peek_real_time_recency(\n        &amp;self,\n        session: &amp;Session,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/optimize.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 378,
            "column": 33
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl MaybeShouldPanic for OptimizerError {\n    fn should_panic(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            OptimizerError::TransformError(TransformError::CallerShouldPanic(msg)) =&gt; {\n                Some(msg.to_string())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Tracing helpers\n// ---------------\n\n#[mz_ore::instrument(target = \"optimizer\", level = \"debug\", name = \"local\")]\nfn optimize_mir_local(\n    expr: MirRelationExpr,\n    ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;OptimizedMirRelationExpr, OptimizerError&gt; {\n    #[allow(deprecated)]\n    let optimizer = mz_transform::Optimizer::logical_optimizer(ctx);\n    let expr = optimizer.optimize(expr, ctx)?;\n\n    // Trace the result of this phase.\n    mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(expr.as_inner());\n\n    Ok::&lt;_, OptimizerError&gt;(expr)\n}\n\n/// This is just a wrapper around [mz_transform::Optimizer::constant_optimizer],\n/// running it, and tracing the result plan.\n#[mz_ore::instrument(target = \"optimizer\", level = \"debug\", name = \"constant\")]\nfn optimize_mir_constant(\n    expr: MirRelationExpr,\n    ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    limit: bool,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;MirRelationExpr, OptimizerError&gt; {\n    let optimizer = mz_transform::Optimizer::constant_optimizer(ctx, limit);\n    let expr = optimizer.optimize(expr, ctx)?;\n\n    // Trace the result of this phase.\n    mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(expr.as_inner());\n\n    Ok::&lt;_, OptimizerError&gt;(expr.0)\n}\n\nmacro_rules! trace_plan {\n    (at: $span:literal, $plan:expr) =&gt; {\n        tracing::debug_span!(target: \"optimizer\", $span).in_scope(|| {\n            mz_repr::explain::trace_plan($plan);\n        });\n    }\n}\n\nuse trace_plan;\n\nuse crate::coord::ExplainContext;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/error.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1059,
            "column": 13
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1125,
            "column": 40
          }
        },
        "contents": "            AdapterError::UnavailableFeature { feature, docs } =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"{} is not supported in this environment.\", feature)?;\n                if let Some(docs) = docs {\n                    write!(\n                        f,\n                        \" For more information consult the documentation at {docs}\"\n                    )?;\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            AdapterError::AlterClusterWhilePendingReplicas =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"cannot alter clusters with pending updates\")\n            }\n            AdapterError::ReplacementSchemaMismatch(_) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"replacement schema differs from target schema\")\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;anyhow::Error&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: anyhow::Error) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e.downcast::&lt;PlanError&gt;() {\n            Ok(plan_error) =&gt; AdapterError::PlanError(plan_error),\n            Err(e) =&gt; AdapterError::Unstructured(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TryFromIntError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: TryFromIntError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Unstructured(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TryFromDecimalError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: TryFromDecimalError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Unstructured(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_catalog::memory::error::Error&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_catalog::memory::error::Error) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Catalog(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_catalog::durable::CatalogError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_catalog::durable::CatalogError) -&gt; Self {\n        mz_catalog::memory::error::Error::from(e).into()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_catalog::durable::DurableCatalogError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_catalog::durable::DurableCatalogError) -&gt; Self {\n        mz_catalog::durable::CatalogError::from(e).into()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;EvalError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: EvalError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Eval(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;ExplainError&gt; for AdapterError",

        "contents": "        Ok(StageResult::Handle(mz_ore::task::spawn_blocking(\n            || \"optimize peek\",\n            move || {\n                span.in_scope(|| {\n                    let pipeline = || -&gt; Result&lt;Either&lt;optimize::peek::GlobalLirPlan, optimize::copy_to::GlobalLirPlan&gt;, AdapterError&gt; {\n                        let _dispatch_guard = explain_ctx.dispatch_guard();\n\n                        let raw_expr = plan.source.clone();\n\n                        match optimizer.as_mut() {\n                            // Optimize SELECT statement.\n                            Either::Left(optimizer) =&gt; {\n                                // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR optimization (local)\n                                let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(raw_expr)?;\n                                // Attach resolved context required to continue the pipeline.\n                                let local_mir_plan = local_mir_plan.resolve(timestamp_context.clone(), &amp;session, stats);\n                                // MIR optimization (global), MIR ⇒ LIR lowering, and LIR optimization (global)\n                                let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan)?;\n\n                                Ok(Either::Left(global_lir_plan))\n                            }\n                            // Optimize COPY TO statement.\n                            Either::Right(optimizer) =&gt; {\n                                // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR optimization (local)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/transform/src/lib.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 255
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 308,
            "column": 40
          }
        },
        "contents": "    /// and one wants to judge progress before some defect occurs.\n    fn debug(&amp;self) -&gt; String {\n        format!(\"{:?}\", self)\n    }\n\n    /// A short string naming the transform, as it will be reported in metrics.\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str;\n}\n\n/// Errors that can occur during a transformation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum TransformError {\n    /// An unstructured error.\n    Internal(String),\n    /// A reference to an apparently unbound identifier.\n    IdentifierMissing(mz_expr::LocalId),\n    /// Notify the caller to panic with the given message.\n    ///\n    /// This is used to bypass catch_unwind-wrapped calls of the optimizer and\n    /// support &#96;SELECT mz_unsafe.mz_panic(&lt;literal&gt;)&#96; statements as a mechanism to kill\n    /// environmentd in various tests.\n    CallerShouldPanic(String),\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for TransformError {\n    fn fmt(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut fmt::Formatter) -&gt; fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            TransformError::Internal(msg) =&gt; write!(f, \"internal transform error: {}\", msg),\n            TransformError::IdentifierMissing(i) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"apparently unbound identifier: {:?}\", i)\n            }\n            TransformError::CallerShouldPanic(msg) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"caller should panic with message: {}\", msg)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Error for TransformError {}\n\nimpl From&lt;RecursionLimitError&gt; for TransformError {\n    fn from(error: RecursionLimitError) -&gt; Self {\n        TransformError::Internal(error.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Implemented by error types that sometimes want to indicate that an error should cause a panic\n/// even in a &#96;catch_unwind&#96; context. Useful for implementing &#96;mz_unsafe.mz_panic('forced panic')&#96;.\npub trait MaybeShouldPanic {\n    /// Whether the error means that we want a panic. If yes, then returns the error msg.\n    fn should_panic(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt;;\n}\n\nimpl MaybeShouldPanic for TransformError",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "contents": "        let optimization_future = mz_ore::task::spawn_blocking(\n            || \"optimize peek\",\n            move || {\n                span.in_scope(|| {\n                    let _dispatch_guard = explain_ctx.dispatch_guard();\n\n                    let raw_expr = select_plan.source.clone();\n\n                    // The purpose of wrapping the following in a closure is to control where the\n                    // &#96;?&#96;s return from, so that even when a &#96;catch_unwind_optimize&#96; call fails,\n                    // we can still handle &#96;EXPLAIN BROKEN&#96;.\n                    let pipeline = || -&gt; Result&lt;Either&lt;optimize::peek::GlobalLirPlan, optimize::copy_to::GlobalLirPlan&gt;, OptimizerError&gt; {\n                        match optimizer.as_mut() {\n                            Either::Left(optimizer) =&gt; {\n                                // SELECT/EXPLAIN path\n                                // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR optimization (local)\n                                let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(raw_expr.clone())?;\n                                // Attach resolved context required to continue the pipeline.\n                                let local_mir_plan =\n                                    local_mir_plan.resolve(timestamp_context.clone(), &amp;session_meta, stats);\n                                // MIR optimization (global), MIR ⇒ LIR lowering, and LIR optimization (global)\n                                let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan)?;\n                                Ok(Either::Left(global_lir_plan))\n                            }\n                            Either::Right(optimizer) =&gt; {\n                                // COPY TO path",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/frontend_peek.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1014,
            "column": 34
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1049,
            "column": 36
          }
        },
        "contents": " *session.vars().statement_timeout();\n        let optimization_result =\n            // Note: spawn_blocking tasks cannot be cancelled, so on timeout we stop waiting but the\n            // optimization task continues running in the background until completion. See\n            // https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/8644 for properly cancelling\n            // optimizer runs.\n            match tokio::time::timeout(optimization_timeout, optimization_future).await {\n                Ok(Ok(result)) =&gt; result,\n                Ok(Err(optimizer_error)) =&gt; {\n                    return Err(AdapterError::Internal(format!(\n                        \"internal error in optimizer: {}\",\n                        optimizer_error\n                    )));\n                }\n                Err(_elapsed) =&gt; {\n                    warn!(\"optimize peek timed out after {:?}\", optimization_timeout);\n                    return Err(AdapterError::StatementTimeout);\n                }\n            };\n\n        // Log optimization finished\n        if let Some(logging_id) = &amp;statement_logging_id {\n            self.log_lifecycle_event(*logging_id, StatementLifecycleEvent::OptimizationFinished);\n        }\n\n        // Assert that read holds are correct for the execution plan\n        Self::assert_read_holds_correct(\n            &amp;read_holds,\n            &amp;optimization_result,\n            &amp;determination,\n            target_cluster_id,\n        );\n\n        // Handle the optimization result: either generate EXPLAIN output or continue with execution\n        match optimization_result {\n            Execution::ExplainPlan {",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": " *session.vars().statement_timeout();",
            "lineNumber": 1015
          },
          {
            "text": "        let optimization_result =",
            "lineNumber": 1016
          },
          {
            "text": "            // Note: spawn_blocking tasks cannot be cancelled, so on timeout we stop waiting but the",
            "lineNumber": 1017
          },
          {
            "text": "            // optimization task continues running in the background until completion. See",
            "lineNumber": 1018
          },
          {
            "text": "            // https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/8644 for properly cancelling",
            "lineNumber": 1019
          },

            "text": "            // optimizer runs.",

          {
            "text": "            match tokio::time::timeout(optimization_timeout, optimization_future).await {",

            "line": 547,
            "column": 57
          }
        },
        "contents": " self.transient_id_gen.allocate_id();\n\n        let mut optimizer = if let Some(mut copy_to_ctx) = copy_to_ctx {\n            // COPY TO path: calculate output_batch_count and create copy_to optimizer\n            let worker_counts = cluster.replicas().map(|r| {\n                let loc = &amp;r.config.location;\n                loc.workers().unwrap_or_else(|| loc.num_processes())\n            });\n            let max_worker_count = match worker_counts.max() {\n                Some(count) =&gt; u64::cast_from(count),\n                None =&gt; {\n                    return Err(AdapterError::NoClusterReplicasAvailable {\n                        name: cluster.name.clone(),\n                        is_managed: cluster.is_managed(),\n                    });\n                }\n            };\n            copy_to_ctx.output_batch_count = Some(max_worker_count);\n\n            Either::Right(optimize::copy_to::Optimizer::new(\n                Arc::clone(&amp;catalog),\n                compute_instance_snapshot.clone(),\n                view_id,\n                copy_to_ctx,\n                optimizer_config,\n                self.optimizer_metrics.clone(),\n            ))\n        } else {\n            // SELECT/EXPLAIN path: create peek optimizer\n            Either::Left(optimize::peek::Optimizer::new(\n                Arc::clone(&amp;catalog),\n                compute_instance_snapshot.clone(),\n                select_plan.finishing.clone(),\n                view_id,\n                index_id,\n                optimizer_config,\n                self.optimizer_metrics.clone(),\n            ))\n        };\n\n        let target_replica_name = session.vars().cluster_replica();\n        let mut target_replica = target_replica_name\n            .map(|name| {\n                cluster\n                    .replica_id(name)\n                    .ok_or(AdapterError::UnknownClusterReplica {\n                        cluster_name: cluster.name.clone(),\n                        replica_name: name.to_string(),\n                    })\n            })\n            .transpose()?;\n\n        let source_ids = select_plan.source.depends_on();",

        "contents": "        let mut optimizer = optimize::materialized_view::Optimizer::new(\n            self.owned_catalog().as_optimizer_catalog(),\n            compute_instance,\n            global_id,\n            view_id,\n            column_names.clone(),\n            non_null_assertions.clone(),\n            refresh_schedule.clone(),\n            debug_name,\n            optimizer_config,\n            self.optimizer_metrics(),\n            force_non_monotonic,\n        );\n\n        let span = Span::current();\n        Ok(StageResult::Handle(mz_ore::task::spawn_blocking(\n            || \"optimize create materialized view\",\n            move || {\n                span.in_scope(|| {\n                    let mut pipeline = || -&gt; Result&lt;(\n                        optimize::materialized_view::LocalMirPlan,\n                        optimize::materialized_view::GlobalMirPlan,\n                        optimize::materialized_view::GlobalLirPlan,\n                    ), AdapterError&gt; {\n                        let _dispatch_guard = explain_ctx.dispatch_guard();\n\n                        let raw_expr = plan.materialized_view.expr.clone();\n\n                        // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR ⇒ MIR optimization (local and global)\n                        let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(raw_expr)?;\n                        let global_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan.clone())?;\n                        // MIR ⇒ LIR lowering and LIR ⇒ LIR optimization (global)\n                        let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(global_mir_plan.clone())?;\n\n                        Ok((local_mir_plan, global_mir_plan, global_lir_plan))\n                    };\n\n                    let stage =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/coord/sequencer/inner/create_continual_task.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 162,
            "column": 5
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 212,
            "column": 70
          }
        },
        "contents": "    pub fn optimize_create_continual_task(\n        &amp;self,\n        ct: &amp;ContinualTask,\n        output_id: GlobalId,\n        catalog: Arc&lt;dyn OptimizerCatalog&gt;,\n        debug_name: String,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;\n        (\n            DataflowDescription&lt;OptimizedMirRelationExpr&gt;,\n            DataflowDescription&lt;Plan&gt;,\n            DataflowMetainfo&lt;Arc&lt;OptimizerNotice&gt;&gt;,\n        ),\n        AdapterError,\n    &gt; {\n        let catalog = Arc::new(NoIndexCatalog { delegate: catalog });\n\n        let (_, view_id) = self.allocate_transient_id();\n        let compute_instance = self\n            .instance_snapshot(ct.cluster_id)\n            .expect(\"compute instance does not exist\");\n        let optimizer_config = optimize::OptimizerConfig::from(self.catalog().system_config())\n            .override_from(&amp;self.catalog.get_cluster(ct.cluster_id).config.features());\n        let non_null_assertions = Vec::new();\n        let refresh_schedule = None;\n        // Continual Tasks turn an \"input\" into diffs by inserting retractions,\n        // which removes any monotonicity properties the collection otherwise\n        // would have had.\n        let force_non_monotonic = [ct.input_id].into();\n        let mut optimizer = optimize::materialized_view::Optimizer::new(\n            catalog,\n            compute_instance,\n            output_id,\n            view_id,\n            ct.desc.iter_names().cloned().collect(),\n            non_null_assertions,\n            refresh_schedule,\n            debug_name,\n            optimizer_config,\n            self.optimizer_metrics(),\n            force_non_monotonic,\n        );\n\n        // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR ⇒ MIR optimization (local and global)\n        let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize((*ct.raw_expr).clone())?;\n        let global_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan)?;\n        let optimized_plan = global_mir_plan.df_desc().clone();\n        // MIR ⇒ LIR lowering and LIR ⇒ LIR optimization (global)\n        let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(global_mir_plan)?;\n        let (mut physical_plan, metainfo) = global_lir_plan.unapply();",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    pub fn optimize_create_continual_task(",

            "lineNumber": 178

            "text": "        let catalog = Arc::new(NoIndexCatalog { delegate: catalog });",

          {
            "lineNumber": 180
          },

            "text": "        let (_, view_id) = self.allocate_transient_id();",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "        let compute_instance = self",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "            .instance_snapshot(ct.cluster_id)",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "            .expect(\"compute instance does not exist\");",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "        let optimizer_config = optimize::OptimizerConfig::from(self.catalog().system_config())",
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "text": "            .override_from(&amp;self.catalog.get_cluster(ct.cluster_id).config.features());",
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "        let non_null_assertions = Vec::new();",
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "text": "        let refresh_schedule = None;",
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "text": "        // Continual Tasks turn an \"input\" into diffs by inserting retractions,",
            "lineNumber": 189

            "text": "        // which removes any monotonicity properties the collection otherwise",

            "text": "        // would have had.",

          },
          {
            "text": "        let mut optimizer = optimize::materialized_view::Optimizer::new(",
            "lineNumber": 193
          },
          {
            "text": "            catalog,",
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "text": "            compute_instance,",
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "text": "            output_id,",
            "lineNumber": 196
          },
          {
            "text": "            view_id,",
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "text": "            ct.desc.iter_names().cloned().collect(),",
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "text": "            non_null_assertions,",
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "text": "            refresh_schedule,",
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "text": "            debug_name,",
            "lineNumber": 201
          },
          {
            "text": "            optimizer_config,",
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "text": "            self.optimizer_metrics(),",
            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "text": "            force_non_monotonic,",
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "text": "        );",
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206
          },
          {
            "text": "        // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR ⇒ MIR optimization (local and global)",
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "text": "        let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize((*ct.raw_expr).clone())?;",
            "lineNumber": 208
          },
          {
            "text": "        let global_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan)?;",
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "text": "        let optimized_plan = global_mir_plan.df_desc().clone();",
            "lineNumber": 210
          },
          {
            "text": "        // MIR ⇒ LIR lowering and LIR ⇒ LIR optimization (global)",
            "lineNumber": 211
          },
          {
            "text": "        let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(global_mir_plan)?;",
            "lineNumber": 212
          },
          {
            "text": "        let (mut physical_plan, metainfo) = global_lir_plan.unapply();",
            "lineNumber": 213
          }
        ]
      },

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/optimize.rs",

          }
        },
        "contents": "// Alias types\n// -----------\n\n/// A type for a [&#96;DataflowDescription&#96;] backed by &#96;Mir~&#96; plans. Used internally\n/// by the optimizer implementations.\ntype MirDataflowDescription = DataflowDescription&lt;OptimizedMirRelationExpr&gt;;\n/// A type for a [&#96;DataflowDescription&#96;] backed by &#96;Lir~&#96; plans. Used internally\n/// by the optimizer implementations.\ntype LirDataflowDescription = DataflowDescription&lt;Plan&gt;;\n\n// Core API\n// --------\n\n/// A trait that represents an optimization stage.\n///\n/// The trait is implemented by structs that encapsulate the context needed to\n/// run an end-to-end optimization pipeline for a specific statement type\n/// (&#96;Index&#96;, &#96;View&#96;, &#96;MaterializedView&#96;, &#96;Subscribe&#96;, &#96;Select&#96;).\n///\n/// Each implementation represents a concrete optimization stage for a fixed\n/// statement type that consumes an input of type &#96;From&#96; and produces output of\n/// type &#96;Self::To&#96;.\npub trait Optimize&lt;From&gt; {\n    type To;\n\n    /// Execute the optimization stage, transforming the input plan of type\n    /// &#96;From&#96; to an output plan of type &#96;To&#96;.\n    fn optimize(&amp;mut self, plan: From) -&gt; Result&lt;Self::To, OptimizerError&gt;;\n\n    /// Like [&#96;Self::optimize&#96;], but additionally ensures that panics occurring\n    /// in the [&#96;Self::optimize&#96;] call are caught and demoted to an\n    /// [&#96;OptimizerError::Internal&#96;] error.\n    ///\n    /// Additionally, if the result of the optimization is an error (not a panic) that indicates we\n    /// should panic, then panic.\n    #[mz_ore::instrument(target = \"optimizer\", level = \"debug\", name = \"optimize\")]\n    fn catch_unwind_optimize(&amp;mut self, plan: From) -&gt; Result&lt;Self::To, OptimizerError&gt; {\n        mz_transform::catch_unwind_optimize(|| self.optimize(plan))\n    }\n}\n\n// Optimizer configuration\n// -----------------------\n\n/// Feature flags for the optimizer.\n///\n/// To add a new feature flag, do the following steps:\n///\n/// 1. To make the flag available to all stages in our [&#96;Optimize&#96;] pipelines\n///    and allow engineers to set a system-wide override:\n///    1. Add the flag to the &#96;optimizer_feature_flags!(...)&#96; macro call.\n///    2. Add the flag to the &#96;feature_flags!(...)&#96; macro call and extend the",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/util.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 311,
            "column": 54
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl&lt;T, E&gt; ResultExt&lt;T&gt; for Result&lt;T, E&gt;\nwhere\n    E: ShouldTerminateGracefully + Debug,\n{\n    fn unwrap_or_terminate(self, context: &amp;str) -&gt; T {\n        match self {\n            Ok(t) =&gt; t,\n            Err(e) if e.should_terminate_gracefully() =&gt; exit!(0, \"{context}: {e:?}\"),\n            Err(e) =&gt; panic!(\"{context}: {e:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn maybe_terminate(self, context: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        if let Err(e) = &amp;self {\n            if e.should_terminate_gracefully() {\n                exit!(0, \"{context}: {e:?}\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// A trait for errors that should terminate gracefully rather than panic\n/// the process.\ntrait ShouldTerminateGracefully {\n    /// Reports whether the error should terminate the process gracefully\n    /// rather than panic.\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool;\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for AdapterError {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            AdapterError::Catalog(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for mz_catalog::memory::error::Error {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match &amp;self.kind {\n            mz_catalog::memory::error::ErrorKind::Durable(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for mz_catalog::durable::CatalogError {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match &amp;self {\n            Self::Durable(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for DurableCatalogError",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "impl&lt;T, E&gt; ResultExt&lt;T&gt; for Result&lt;T, E&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 254

plan\n                                            .desc\n                                            .source_imports\n                                            .into_iter()\n                                            .filter_map(|(id, import)| {\n                                                import.desc\n                                                    .arguments\n                                                    .operators\n                                                    .map(|mfp| (id, mfp))\n                                            })\n                                            .collect(),\n                                        PeekPlan::FastPath(_) =&gt; BTreeMap::default(),\n                                    };\n                                    PeekStage::ExplainPushdown(PeekStageExplainPushdown {\n                                        validity,\n                                        determination,\n                                        imports,\n                                    })\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                        Ok(Either::Right(global_lir_plan)) =&gt; {\n                            let optimizer = optimizer.unwrap_right();\n                            PeekStage::CopyToPreflight(PeekStageCopyTo {\n                                validity,\n                                optimizer,\n                                global_lir_plan,\n                                optimization_finished_at,\n                                source_ids,\n                            })\n                        }\n                        // Internal optimizer errors are handled differently\n                        // depending on the caller.\n                        Err(err) =&gt;         "contents": "pub enum AdapterError {\n    /// A &#96;SUBSCRIBE&#96; was requested whose &#96;UP TO&#96; bound precedes its &#96;as_of&#96; timestamp\n    AbsurdSubscribeBounds {\n        as_of: mz_repr::Timestamp,\n        up_to: mz_repr::Timestamp,\n    },\n    /// Attempted to use a potentially ambiguous column reference expression with a system table.\n    // We don't allow this until https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/4824 is\n    // resolved because it prevents us from adding columns to system tables.\n    AmbiguousSystemColumnReference,\n    /// An error occurred in a catalog operation.\n    Catalog(mz_catalog::memory::error::Error),\n    /// 1. The cached plan or descriptor changed,\n    /// 2. or some dependency of a statement disappeared during sequencing.\n    /// TODO(ggevay): we should refactor 2. usages to use &#96;ConcurrentDependencyDrop&#96; instead\n    /// (e.g., in MV sequencing)\n    ChangedPlan(String),\n    /// The cursor already exists.\n    DuplicateCursor(String),\n    /// An error while evaluating an expression.\n    Eval(EvalError),\n    /// An error occurred while planning the statement.\n    Explain(ExplainError),\n    /// The ID allocator exhausted all valid IDs.\n    IdExhaustionError,\n    /// Unexpected internal state was encountered.\n    Internal(String),\n    /// Attempted to read from log sources of a replica with disabled introspection.\n    IntrospectionDisabled {\n        log_names: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// Attempted to create an object dependent on log sources that doesn't support\n    /// log dependencies.\n    InvalidLogDependency {\n        object_type: String,\n        log_names: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// No such cluster replica size has been configured.\n    InvalidClusterReplicaAz {\n        az: String,\n        expected: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL was called in the middle of a transaction.\n    InvalidSetIsolationLevel,\n    /// SET cluster was called in the middle of a transaction.\n    InvalidSetCluster,\n    /// No such storage instance size has been configured.",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "                mz_ore::task::spawn_blocking(\n                    || \"compute fast path clusters\",\n                    move || {\n                        // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and MIR optimization (local)\n                        let local_mir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(raw_expr)?;\n                        // Attach resolved context required to continue the pipeline.\n                        let local_mir_plan = local_mir_plan.resolve(\n                            timestamp_context,\n                            &amp;*session,\n                            Box::new(EmptyStatisticsOracle {}),\n                        );\n                        // MIR optimization (global), MIR ⇒ LIR lowering, and LIR optimization (global)\n                        let global_lir_plan = optimizer.catch_unwind_optimize(local_mir_plan)?;\n                        Ok::&lt;_, OptimizerError&gt;((name, global_lir_plan))\n                    },\n                )\n            });\n        for task in tasks {\n            let res = task.await;",

      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 227,

        "contents": ",\n}\n\nimpl Optimize&lt;HirRelationExpr&gt; for Optimizer {\n    type To = LocalMirPlan;\n\n    fn optimize(&amp;mut self, expr: HirRelationExpr) -&gt; Result&lt;Self::To, OptimizerError&gt; {\n        let time = Instant::now();\n\n        // Trace the pipeline input under &#96;optimize/raw&#96;.\n        trace_plan!(at: \"raw\", &amp;expr);\n\n        // HIR ⇒ MIR lowering and decorrelation\n        let mir_expr = expr.clone().lower(&amp;self.config, Some(&amp;self.metrics))?;\n\n        // MIR ⇒ MIR optimization (local)\n        let mut df_meta = DataflowMetainfo::default();\n        let mut transform_ctx = TransformCtx::local(\n            &amp;self.config.features,\n            &amp;self.repr_typecheck_ctx,\n            &amp;mut df_meta,\n            Some(&amp;mut self.metrics),\n            Some(self.select_id),\n        );\n        let mir_expr = optimize_mir_local(mir_expr, &amp;mut transform_ctx)?.into_inner();\n        let typ = infer_sql_type_for_catalog(&amp;expr, &amp;mir_expr);\n\n        self.duration += time.elapsed();\n\n        // Return the (sealed) plan at the end of this optimization step.\n        Ok(LocalMirPlan {\n            expr: mir_expr,\n            typ,\n            df_meta,\n            context: Unresolved,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl LocalMirPlan&lt;Unresolved&gt; {\n    /// Produces the [&#96;LocalMirPlan&#96;] with [&#96;Resolved&#96;] contextual information\n    /// required for the next stage.\n    pub fn resolve(\n        self,\n        timestamp_ctx: TimestampContext&lt;Timestamp&gt;,\n        session: &amp;dyn SessionMetadata,\n        stats: Box&lt;dyn StatisticsOracle&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; LocalMirPlan&lt;Resolved&lt;'_&gt;&gt; {\n        LocalMirPlan {\n            expr: self.expr,\n            typ: self.typ,\n            df_meta: self.df_meta,\n            context: Resolved {\n                timestamp_ctx,\n                session,\n                stats,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'s&gt; Optimize&lt;LocalMirPlan&lt;Resolved&lt;'s&gt;&gt;&gt; for Optimizer",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/transform/src/lib.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 973,
            "column": 6
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1014,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "    pub fn optimize(\n        &amp;self,\n        mut relation: MirRelationExpr,\n        ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;mz_expr::OptimizedMirRelationExpr, TransformError&gt; {\n        let transform_result = self.transform(&amp;mut relation, ctx);\n\n        match transform_result {\n            Ok(_) =&gt; {\n                mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(&amp;relation);\n                Ok(mz_expr::OptimizedMirRelationExpr(relation))\n            }\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                // Without this, the dropping of &#96;relation&#96; (which happens automatically when\n                // returning from this function) might run into a stack overflow, see\n                // https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/4043\n                relation.destroy_carefully();\n                error!(\"Optimizer::optimize(): {}\", e);\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Optimizes the supplied relation expression in place, using available arrangements.\n    ///\n    /// This method should only be called with non-empty &#96;indexes&#96; when optimizing a dataflow,\n    /// as the optimizations may lock in the use of arrangements that may cease to exist.\n    fn transform(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        args: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), TransformError&gt; {\n        args.update_last_hash(relation);\n\n        for transform in self.transforms.iter() {\n            transform.transform(relation, args)?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 975
          },

            "text": "        &amp;self,",

          },

            "lineNumber": 980
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 981

            "text": "        match transform_result {",

            "lineNumber": 985

            "lineNumber": 989

            "text": "                // https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/4043",

            "text": "                relation.destroy_carefully();",
            "lineNumber": 991
          },
          {
            "text": "                error!(\"Optimizer::optimize(): {}\", e);",
            "lineNumber": 992
          },
          {
            "text": "                Err(e)",
            "lineNumber": 993
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 994

          {
            "text": "        }",

            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 996
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 997
          },
          {
            "text": "    /// Optimizes the supplied relation expression in place, using available arrangements.",

If an error is returned then the error is sent\n/// to the client and the function is exited.\nmacro_rules! return_if_err {\n    ($expr:expr, $ctx:expr) =&gt; {\n        match $expr {\n            Ok(v) =&gt; v,\n            Err(e) =&gt; return $ctx.retire(Err(e.into())),\n        }\n    };\n}\n\npub(super) use return_if_err;\n\nstruct DropOps {\n    ops: Vec&lt;catalog::Op&gt;,\n    dropped_active_db: bool,",

          }
        },
        "contents": " {\n        &amp;self.recursion_guard\n    }\n}\n\nimpl crate::Transform for LiteralLifting {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        \"LiteralLifting\"\n    }\n\n    #[mz_ore::instrument(\n        target = \"optimizer\",\n        level = \"debug\",\n        fields(path.segment = \"literal_lifting\")\n    )]\n    fn actually_perform_transform(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        _: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), crate::TransformError&gt; {\n        let literals = self.action(relation, &amp;mut BTreeMap::new())?;\n        if !literals.is_empty() {\n            // Literals return up the root should be re-installed.\n            *relation = relation.take_dangerous().map(literals);\n        }\n        mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(&amp;*relation);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl LiteralLifting {\n    /// Hoist literal values from maps wherever possible.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a list of literal scalar expressions that must be appended\n    /// to the result before it can be correctly used. The intent is that\n    /// this action extracts a maximal set of literals from &#96;relation&#96;,\n    /// which can then often be propagated further up and inlined in any\n    /// expressions as it goes.\n    ///\n    /// In several cases, we only manage to extract literals from the final\n    /// columns. But in those cases where it is possible, permutations are\n    /// used to move all of the literals to the final columns, and then rely\n    /// on projection hoisting to allow the these literals to move up the AST.\n    ///\n    /// TODO: The literals from the final columns are returned as the result\n    /// of this method, whereas literals in intermediate columns are extracted\n    /// using permutations. The reason for this different treatment is that in\n    /// some cases it is not possible to remove the projection of the\n    /// permutation, preventing the lifting of a literal that could otherwise\n    /// be lifted, the following example being of them:\n    ///\n    /// %0 =\n    /// | Constant (1, 2, 3) (2, 2, 3)\n    ///\n    /// %1 =\n    /// | Constant (4, 3, 3) (4, 5, 3)\n    ///\n    /// %2 =\n    /// | Union %0 %1",

        "contents": "/// &#96;purify_statement&#96;. Feel free to rationalize this by thinking of those\n/// statements as not necessarily depending on external state.\n#[mz_ore::instrument(level = \"debug\")]\npub fn plan(\n    pcx: Option&lt;&amp;PlanContext&gt;,\n    catalog: &amp;dyn SessionCatalog,\n    stmt: Statement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    params: &amp;Params,\n    resolved_ids: &amp;ResolvedIds,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Plan, PlanError&gt; {\n    let param_types = params\n        // We need the &#96;expected_types&#96; here, not the &#96;actual_types&#96;! This is because\n        // &#96;expected_types&#96; is how the parameter expression (e.g. &#96;$1&#96;) looks \"from the outside\":\n        // &#96;bind_parameters&#96; will insert a cast from the actual type to the expected type.\n        .expected_types\n        .iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .map(|(i, ty)| (i + 1, ty.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let kind: StatementKind = (&amp;stmt).into();\n    let permitted_plans = Plan::generated_from(&amp;kind);\n\n    let scx = &amp;mut StatementContext {\n        pcx,\n        catalog,\n        param_types: RefCell::new(param_types),\n        ambiguous_columns: RefCell::new(false),\n    };\n\n    if resolved_ids\n        .items()\n        // Filter out items that may not have been created yet, such as sub-sources.\n        .filter_map(|id| catalog.try_get_item(id))\n        .any(|item| {\n            item.func().is_ok()\n                &amp;&amp; item.name().qualifiers.schema_spec\n                    == SchemaSpecifier::Id(catalog.get_mz_unsafe_schema_id())\n        })\n    {\n        scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::UNSAFE_ENABLE_UNSAFE_FUNCTIONS)?;\n    }\n\n    let plan = match stmt {\n        // DDL statements.",

        "contents": "            let value = dataflow.objects_to_build[index]\n                .plan\n                .as_inner_mut()\n                .take_dangerous();\n            *dataflow.objects_to_build[other].plan.as_inner_mut() = MirRelationExpr::Let {\n                id: new_local,\n                value: Box::new(value),\n                body: Box::new(body),\n            };\n            dataflow.objects_to_build.remove(index);\n        }\n    }\n\n    mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(dataflow);\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Performs either the logical or the physical optimization pass on the\n/// dataflow using the supplied set of indexes.\n#[mz_ore::instrument(\n    target = \"optimizer\",\n    level = \"debug\",\n    fields(path.segment = optimizer.name)\n)]\nfn optimize_dataflow_relations(\n    dataflow: &amp;mut DataflowDesc,\n    optimizer: &amp;Optimizer,\n    ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(), TransformError&gt; {\n    // Re-optimize each dataflow\n    for object in dataflow.objects_to_build.iter_mut() {\n        // Re-run all optimizations on the composite views.\n        ctx.set_global_id(object.id);\n        optimizer.transform(object.plan.as_inner_mut(), ctx)?;\n        ctx.reset_global_id();\n    }\n\n    mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(dataflow);\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Pushes demand information from published outputs to dataflow inputs,\n/// projecting away unnecessary columns.\n///\n/// Dataflows that exist for the sake of generating plan explanations do not\n/// have published outputs. In this case, we push demand information from views\n/// not depended on by other views to dataflow inputs.\n#[mz_ore::instrument(\n    target = \"optimizer\",\n    level = \"debug\",\n    fields(path.segment =\"demand\")\n)]\nfn optimize_dataflow_demand(dataflow: &amp;mut DataflowDesc) -&gt; Result&lt;(), TransformError&gt; {\n    // Maps id -&gt; union of known columns demanded from the source/view with the",

            "text": "/// projecting away unnecessary columns.",

          },
          {
            "text": "///",
            "lineNumber": 242
          },
          {
            "text": "/// Dataflows that exist for the sake of generating plan explanations do not",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 251
          },

            "text": "    // Maps id -&gt; union of known columns demanded from the source/view with the",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/transform/src/column_knowledge.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 536,
