        },
        "contents": "                }\n                MirRelationExpr::Union { base, inputs } =&gt; {\n                    let mut know = self.harvest(base, knowledge, knowledge_stack)?;\n                    for input in inputs {\n                        know = know\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .zip_eq(self.harvest(input, knowledge, knowledge_stack)?)\n                            .map(|(mut k1, k2)| {\n                                k1.join_assign(&amp;k2);\n                                k1\n                            })\n                            .collect();\n                    }\n                    Ok(know)\n                }\n            }?;\n\n            // println!(\"# Plan\");\n            // println!(\"{}\", expr.pretty());\n            // println!(\"# Knowledge\");\n            // print_knowledge_vec(&amp;result);\n            // println!(\"---\");\n\n            Ok(result)\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Information about a specific column.\n///\n/// The values should form a [complete lattice].\n///\n/// [complete lattice]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Complete_lattice\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum DatumKnowledge {\n    // Any possible value, optionally known to be NOT NULL.\n    Any {\n        nullable: bool,\n    },\n    // A known literal value of a specific type.\n    Lit {\n        value: Result&lt;mz_repr::Row, EvalError&gt;,\n        typ: SqlScalarType,\n    },\n    // A value that cannot exist.\n    Nothing,\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;MirScalarExpr&gt; for DatumKnowledge {\n    fn from(expr: &amp;MirScalarExpr) -&gt; Self {\n        if let MirScalarExpr::Literal(l, t) = expr {\n            let value = l.clone();\n            let typ = t.scalar_type.clone();\n            Self::Lit { value, typ }\n        } else {\n            Self::top()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;(Datum&lt;'_&gt;, &amp;SqlColumnType)&gt; for DatumKnowledge {\n    fn from((d, t): (Datum&lt;'_&gt;, &amp;SqlColumnType)) -&gt; Self {\n        let value = Ok(Row::pack_slice(std::slice::from_ref(&amp;d)));\n        let typ = t.scalar_type.clone();\n        Self::Lit { value, typ }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;SqlColumnType&gt; for DatumKnowledge",

        "contents": " Result&lt;MirScalarExpr, PlanError&gt; {\n        maybe_grow(|| {\n            use MirScalarExpr as SS;\n\n            use HirScalarExpr::*;\n\n            if let Some(subquery_map) = subquery_map {\n                if let Some(col) = subquery_map.get(&amp;self) {\n                    return Ok(SS::column(*col));\n                }\n            }\n\n            Ok::&lt;MirScalarExpr, PlanError&gt;(match self {\n                Column(col_ref, name) =&gt; SS::Column(col_map.get(&amp;col_ref), name),\n                Literal(row, typ, _name) =&gt; SS::Literal(Ok(row), typ),\n                Parameter(_, _name) =&gt; {\n                    panic!(\"cannot decorrelate expression with unbound parameters\")\n                }\n                CallUnmaterializable(func, _name) =&gt; SS::CallUnmaterializable(func),\n                CallUnary {\n                    func: func::UnaryFunc::CastVarCharToString(_),\n                    expr,\n                    name: _,\n                } if context.config.enable_cast_elimination =&gt; {\n                    expr.applied_to(id_gen, col_map, cte_map, inner, subquery_map, context)?\n                }\n                CallUnary {\n                    func,\n                    expr,\n                    name: _,\n                } =&gt; SS::CallUnary {\n                    func,\n                    expr: Box::new(expr.applied_to(\n                        id_gen,\n                        col_map,\n                        cte_map,\n                        inner,\n                        subquery_map,\n                        context,\n                    )?),\n                },\n                CallBinary {\n                    func,\n                    expr1",

        "contents": "pub struct LiteralConstraints;\n\nimpl crate::Transform for LiteralConstraints {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        \"LiteralConstraints\"\n    }\n\n    #[mz_ore::instrument(\n        target = \"optimizer\",\n        level = \"debug\",\n        fields(path.segment = \"literal_constraints\")\n    )]\n    fn actually_perform_transform(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), crate::TransformError&gt; {\n        let result = self.action(relation, ctx);\n        mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(&amp;*relation);\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nimpl LiteralConstraints {\n    fn action(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        transform_ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), crate::TransformError&gt; {\n        let mut mfp = MapFilterProject::extract_non_errors_from_expr_mut(relation);\n        relation.try_visit_mut_children(|e| self.action(e, transform_ctx))?;\n\n        if let MirRelationExpr::Get {\n            id: Id::Global(id),",

            "column": 18
          }
        },
        "contents": "fn plan_expr_inner&lt;'a&gt;(\n    ecx: &amp;'a ExprContext,\n    e: &amp;Expr&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;CoercibleScalarExpr, PlanError&gt; {\n    if let Some((i, item)) = ecx.scope.resolve_expr(e) {\n        // We've already calculated this expression.\n        return Ok(HirScalarExpr::named_column(\n            i,\n            ecx.qcx.name_manager.borrow_mut().intern_scope_item(item),\n        )\n        .into());\n    }\n\n    match e {\n        // Names.\n        Expr::Identifier(names) | Expr::QualifiedWildcard(names) =&gt; {\n            Ok(plan_identifier(ecx, names)?.into())\n        }\n\n        // Literals.\n        Expr::Value(val) =&gt; plan_literal(val),\n        Expr::Parameter(n) =&gt; plan_parameter(ecx, *n),\n        Expr::Array(exprs) =&gt; plan_array(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::List(exprs) =&gt; plan_list(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::Map(exprs) =&gt; plan_map(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::Row { exprs } =&gt; plan_row(ecx, exprs),\n\n        // Generalized functions, operators, and casts.\n        Expr::Op { op, expr1, expr2 } =&gt; {\n            Ok(plan_op(ecx, normalize::op(op)?, expr1, expr2.as_deref())?.into())\n        }\n        Expr::Cast { expr, data_type } =&gt; plan_cast(ecx, expr, data_type),\n        Expr::Function(func) =&gt; Ok(plan_function(ecx, func)?.into()),\n\n        // Special functions and operators.\n        Expr::Not { expr } =&gt; plan_not(ecx, expr),\n        Expr::And { left, right } =&gt; plan_and(ecx, left, right),\n        Expr::Or { left, right } =&gt; plan_or(ecx, left, right),\n        Expr::IsExpr {\n            expr,\n            construct,\n            negated,\n        } =&gt; Ok(plan_is_expr(ecx, expr, construct, *negated)?.into()),\n        Expr::Case {\n            operand,\n            conditions,\n            results,\n            else_result,\n        } =&gt; Ok(plan_case(ecx, operand, conditions, results, else_result)?.into()),\n        Expr::HomogenizingFunction { function, exprs } =&gt; {\n            plan_homogenizing_function(ecx, function, exprs)\n        }\n        Expr::NullIf { l_expr, r_expr } =&gt; Ok(plan_case(\n            ecx,\n            &amp;None,",

        "contents": "        })?;\n        Ok((output, subquery_map))\n    }\n\n    /// Rewrites &#96;self&#96; into a &#96;mz_expr::ScalarExpr&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// Returns an _internal_ error if the expression contains\n    /// - a subquery\n    /// - a column reference to an outer level\n    /// - a parameter\n    /// - a window function call\n    ///\n    /// Should succeed if [&#96;HirScalarExpr::is_constant&#96;] would return true on &#96;self&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// Set &#96;enable_cast_elimination&#96; to remove casts that are noops in MIR.\n    pub fn lower_uncorrelated&lt;C: Into&lt;Config&gt;&gt;(\n        self,\n        config: C,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;MirScalarExpr, PlanError&gt; {\n        let config = config.into();\n\n        use MirScalarExpr as SS;\n\n        use HirScalarExpr::*;\n\n        Ok(match self {\n            Column(ColumnRef { level: 0, column }, name) =&gt; SS::Column(column, name),\n            Literal(datum, typ, _name) =&gt; SS::Literal(Ok(datum), typ),\n            CallUnmaterializable(func, _name) =&gt; SS::CallUnmaterializable(func),\n            CallUnary {\n                func: func::UnaryFunc::CastVarCharToString(_),\n                expr,\n                name: _,\n            } if config.enable_cast_elimination =&gt; expr.lower_uncorrelated(config)?,\n            CallUnary {\n                func,\n                expr,\n                name: _,\n            } =&gt; SS::CallUnary {\n                func,\n                expr: Box::new(expr.lower_uncorrelated(config)?),\n            },\n            CallBinary {\n                func,\n                expr1,\n                expr2,\n                name: _,\n            } =&gt; SS::CallBinary {\n                func,\n                expr1: Box::new(expr1.lower_uncorrelated(config)?),\n                expr2: Box::new(expr2.lower_uncorrelated(config)?),\n            },\n            CallVariadic {\n                func,",

        "contents": "        if let MirRelationExpr::Get {\n            id: Id::Global(id),\n            ref typ,\n            ..\n        } = *relation\n        {\n            let orig_mfp = mfp.clone();\n\n            // Preparation for the literal constraints detection.\n            Self::inline_literal_constraints(&amp;mut mfp);\n            Self::list_of_predicates_to_and_of_predicates(&amp;mut mfp);\n            Self::distribute_and_over_or(&amp;mut mfp)?;\n            Self::unary_and(&amp;mut mfp);\n\n            /// The above preparation might make the MFP more complicated, so we'll later want to\n            /// either undo the preparation transformations or get back to &#96;orig_mfp&#96;.\n            fn undo_preparation(\n                mfp: &amp;mut MapFilterProject,\n                orig_mfp: &amp;MapFilterProject,\n                relation: &amp;MirRelationExpr,\n                relation_type: SqlRelationType,\n            ) {\n                // undo list_of_predicates_to_and_of_predicates, distribute_and_over_or, unary_and\n                // (It undoes the latter 2 through &#96;MirScalarExp::reduce&#96;.)\n                LiteralConstraints::canonicalize_predicates(mfp, relation, relation_type);\n                // undo inline_literal_constraints\n                mfp.optimize();\n                // We can usually undo, but sometimes not (see comment on &#96;distribute_and_over_or&#96;),\n                // so in those cases we might have a more complicated MFP than the original MFP\n                // (despite the removal of the literal constraints and/or contradicting OR args).\n                // So let's use the simpler one.\n                if LiteralConstraints::predicates_size(orig_mfp)\n                    &lt; LiteralConstraints::predicates_size(mfp)\n                {\n                    *mfp = orig_mfp.clone();\n                }\n            }\n\n            let removed_contradicting_or_args = Self::remove_impossible_or_args(&amp;mut mfp)?;\n\n            // todo: We might want to also call &#96;canonicalize_equivalences&#96;,\n            // see near the end of literal_constraints.slt.\n\n            let inp_typ = typ.clone();\n\n            let key_val =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/dml.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 249,
            "column": 21
          }
        },
        "contents": "    Ok(Plan::Select(plan))\n}\n\nfn plan_select_inner(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    select: SelectStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    params: &amp;Params,\n    copy_to: Option&lt;CopyFormat&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(SelectPlan, RelationDesc), PlanError&gt; {\n    let when = query::plan_as_of(scx, select.as_of.clone())?;\n    let lifetime = QueryLifetime::OneShot;\n    let query::PlannedRootQuery {\n        mut expr,\n        desc,\n        finishing,\n        scope: _,\n    } = query::plan_root_query(scx, select.query.clone(), lifetime)?;\n    expr.bind_parameters(scx, lifetime, params)?;\n\n    // OFFSET clauses in &#96;expr&#96; should become constants with the above binding of parameters.\n    // Let's check this and simplify them to literals.\n    expr.try_visit_mut_pre(&amp;mut |expr| {\n        if let HirRelationExpr::TopK { offset, .. } = expr {\n            let offset_value = offset_into_value(offset.take())?;\n            *offset = HirScalarExpr::literal(Datum::Int64(offset_value), SqlScalarType::Int64);\n        }\n        Ok::&lt;(), PlanError&gt;(())\n    })?;\n    // (We don't need to simplify LIMIT clauses in &#96;expr&#96;, because we can handle non-constant\n    // expressions there. If they happen to be simplifiable to literals, then the optimizer will do\n    // so later.)\n\n    // We need to concretize the &#96;limit&#96; and &#96;offset&#96; of the RowSetFinishing, so that we go from\n    // &#96;RowSetFinishing&lt;HirScalarExpr, HirScalarExpr&gt;&#96; to &#96;RowSetFinishing&#96;.\n    // This involves binding parameters and evaluating each expression to a number.\n    // (This should be possible even for &#96;limit&#96; here, because we are at the top level of a SELECT,\n    // so this &#96;limit&#96; has to be a constant.)\n    let limit = match finishing.limit {\n        None =&gt; None,",

        "contents": "        &amp;mut |e| {\n            let result = match e {\n                MirScalarExpr::Column(index, _) =&gt; {\n                    let index = *index;\n                    if let DatumKnowledge::Lit { value, typ } = &amp;column_knowledge[index] {\n                        let nullable = column_knowledge[index].nullable();\n                        *e = MirScalarExpr::Literal(value.clone(), typ.clone().nullable(nullable));\n                    }\n                    column_knowledge[index].clone()\n                }\n                MirScalarExpr::Literal(_, _) | MirScalarExpr::CallUnmaterializable(_) =&gt; {\n                    DatumKnowledge::from(&amp;*e)\n                }\n                MirScalarExpr::CallUnary { func, expr: _ } =&gt; {\n                    let knowledge = knowledge_stack.pop().unwrap();\n                    if matches!(&amp;knowledge, DatumKnowledge::Lit { .. }) {\n                        e.reduce(column_types);\n                    } else if func == &amp;UnaryFunc::IsNull(func::IsNull) &amp;&amp; !knowledge.nullable() {\n                        *e = MirScalarExpr::literal_false();\n                    };\n                    DatumKnowledge::from(&amp;*e)\n                }\n                MirScalarExpr::CallBinary {\n                    func: _,\n                    expr1: _,\n                    expr2: _,\n                } =&gt; {\n                    let knowledge2 = knowledge_stack.pop().unwrap();\n                    let knowledge1 = knowledge_stack.pop().unwrap();\n                    if crate::any![\n                        matches!(knowledge1, DatumKnowledge::Lit { .. }),\n                        matches!(knowledge2, DatumKnowledge::Lit { .. }),\n                    ] {\n                        e.reduce(column_types);\n                    }\n                    DatumKnowledge::from(&amp;*e)\n                }\n                MirScalarExpr::CallVariadic { func: _, exprs } =&gt; {\n                    // Drain the last &#96;exprs.len()&#96; knowledge, and reduce if any is &#96;Lit&#96;.",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "                        if literal_count != 0 {\n                            let input_arity = input.arity();\n                            let first_literal_id = input_arity + scalars.len() - literal_count;\n                            let mut new_scalars = Vec::new();\n                            let mut projected_literals = Vec::new();\n                            let mut projection = (0..input_arity).collect::&lt;Vec&lt;usize&gt;&gt;();\n                            for scalar in scalars.iter_mut() {\n                                if scalar.is_literal() {\n                                    projection.push(first_literal_id + projected_literals.len());\n                                    projected_literals.push(scalar.clone());\n                                } else {\n                                    let mut cloned_scalar = scalar.clone();\n                                    // Propagate literals through expressions and remap columns.\n                                    cloned_scalar.visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n                                        if let MirScalarExpr::Column(old_id, _) = e {\n                                            let new_id = projection[*old_id];\n                                            if new_id &gt;= first_literal_id {\n                                                *e = projected_literals[new_id - first_literal_id]\n                                                    .clone();\n                                            } else {\n                                                *old_id = new_id;\n                                            }\n                                        }\n                                    })?;\n                                    projection.push(input_arity + new_scalars.len());\n                                    new_scalars.push(cloned_scalar);\n                                }\n                            }\n                            new_scalars.extend(projected_literals);\n                            *relation = input.take_dangerous().map(new_scalars).project(projection);\n                        }\n                    }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

        "range": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 348
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl MaybeShouldPanic for TransformError {\n    fn should_panic(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            TransformError::CallerShouldPanic(msg) =&gt; Some(msg.to_string()),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Catch panics in the given optimization, and demote them to [&#96;TransformError::Internal&#96;] error.\n///\n/// Additionally, if the result of the optimization is an error (not a panic) that indicates we\n/// should panic, then panic.\npub fn catch_unwind_optimize&lt;Opt, To, E&gt;(optimization: Opt) -&gt; Result&lt;To, E&gt;\nwhere\n    Opt: FnOnce() -&gt; Result&lt;To, E&gt;,\n    E: From&lt;TransformError&gt; + MaybeShouldPanic,\n{\n    match mz_ore::panic::catch_unwind_str(AssertUnwindSafe(optimization)) {\n        Ok(Err(e)) if e.should_panic().is_some() =&gt; {\n            // Promote a &#96;CallerShouldPanic&#96; error from the result to a proper panic. This is\n            // needed in order to ensure that &#96;mz_unsafe.mz_panic('forced panic')&#96; calls still\n            // panic the caller.\n            panic!(\"{}\", e.should_panic().expect(\"checked above\"));\n        }\n        Ok(result) =&gt; result.map_err(|e| e),\n        Err(panic) =&gt; {\n            // A panic during optimization is always a bug; log an error so we learn about it.\n            // TODO(teskje): collect and log a backtrace from the panic site\n            tracing::error!(\"caught a panic during query optimization: {panic}\");\n\n            let msg = format!(\"unexpected panic during query optimization: {panic}\");\n            Err(TransformError::Internal(msg).into())\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A trait for a type that can answer questions about what indexes exist.\npub trait IndexOracle: fmt::Debug {\n    /// Returns an iterator over the indexes that exist on the identified",

        "contents": "                MirScalarExpr::CallVariadic { func: _, exprs } =&gt; {\n                    // Drain the last &#96;exprs.len()&#96; knowledge, and reduce if any is &#96;Lit&#96;.\n                    assert!(knowledge_stack.len() &gt;= exprs.len());\n                    if knowledge_stack\n                        .drain(knowledge_stack.len() - exprs.len()..)\n                        .any(|k| matches!(k, DatumKnowledge::Lit { .. }))\n                    {\n                        e.reduce(column_types);\n                    }\n                    DatumKnowledge::from(&amp;*e)\n                }\n                MirScalarExpr::If {\n                    cond: _,\n                    then: _,\n                    els: _,\n                } =&gt; {\n                    // &#96;cond&#96; has been left un-optimized, as we should not remove the conditional\n                    // nature of the evaluation based on column knowledge: the resulting\n                    // expression could then move down past a filter or join that provided\n                    // the guarantees, and would become wrong.\n                    //\n                    // Instead, each of the branches have been optimized, and we\n                    // can union the states of their columns.\n                    let know2 = knowledge_stack.pop().unwrap();\n                    let mut know1 = knowledge_stack.pop().unwrap();\n                    know1.join_assign(&amp;know2);\n                    know1\n                }\n            };\n            knowledge_stack.push(result);\n        },\n    )?;\n    let knowledge_datum = knowledge_stack.pop();\n    assert!(knowledge_stack.is_empty());\n    knowledge_datum.ok_or_else(|| {\n        TransformError::Internal(String::from(\"unexpectedly empty stack in optimize\"))\n    })\n}\n\n#[allow(dead_code)] // keep debugging method around\nfn print_knowledge_map&lt;'a&gt;(\n    knowledge:",

        "contents": ";\n\n                if derived_typ.column_types.len() != proposed_typ.column_types.len() {\n                    return type_err(proposed_typ, derived_typ);\n                }\n\n                // Cast derived types to proposed types or error.\n                let val = match cast_relation(\n                    qcx,\n                    // Choose &#96;CastContext::Assignment&#96;&#96; because the user has\n                    // been explicit about the types they expect. Choosing\n                    // &#96;CastContext::Implicit&#96; is not \"strong\" enough to impose\n                    // typmods from proposed types onto values.\n                    CastContext::Assignment,\n                    val,\n                    proposed_typ.column_types.iter().map(|c| &amp;c.scalar_type),\n                ) {\n                    Ok(val) =&gt; val,\n                    Err(_) =&gt; return type_err(proposed_typ, derived_typ),\n                };\n\n                result.push((cte.id, val, shadowed_descs.remove(&amp;cte.id)));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(result)\n}\n\npub fn plan_nested_query(\n    qcx: &amp;mut QueryContext,\n    q: &amp;Query&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(HirRelationExpr, Scope), PlanError&gt; {\n    let PlannedQuery {\n        mut expr,\n        scope,\n        order_by,\n        limit,\n        offset,\n        project,\n        group_size_hints,\n    } = qcx.checked_recur_mut(|qcx| plan_query(qcx, q))?;\n    if limit.is_some()\n        || !offset\n            .clone()\n            .try_into_literal_int64()\n            .is_ok_and(|offset| offset == 0)\n    {\n        expr = HirRelationExpr::top_k(\n            expr,\n            vec![],\n            order_by,\n            limit,\n            offset,\n            group_size_hints.limit_input_group_size,\n        );\n    }\n    Ok((expr.project(project), scope))\n}\n\nfn plan_set_expr(\n    qcx: &amp;mut QueryContext,\n    q: &amp;SetExpr&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt;",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/transform/src/lib.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 973,
            "column": 6
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1014,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "    pub fn optimize(\n        &amp;self,\n        mut relation: MirRelationExpr,\n        ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;mz_expr::OptimizedMirRelationExpr, TransformError&gt; {\n        let transform_result = self.transform(&amp;mut relation, ctx);\n\n        match transform_result {\n            Ok(_) =&gt; {\n                mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(&amp;relation);\n                Ok(mz_expr::OptimizedMirRelationExpr(relation))\n            }\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                // Without this, the dropping of &#96;relation&#96; (which happens automatically when\n                // returning from this function) might run into a stack overflow, see\n                // https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/4043\n                relation.destroy_carefully();\n                error!(\"Optimizer::optimize(): {}\", e);\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Optimizes the supplied relation expression in place, using available arrangements.\n    ///\n    /// This method should only be called with non-empty &#96;indexes&#96; when optimizing a dataflow,\n    /// as the optimizations may lock in the use of arrangements that may cease to exist.\n    fn transform(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        args: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), TransformError&gt; {\n        args.update_last_hash(relation);\n\n        for transform in self.transforms.iter() {\n            transform.transform(relation, args)?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 981

            "lineNumber": 992

          {
            "text": "                Err(e)",
            "lineNumber": 993

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/query.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

            "column": 24
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub fn plan_params&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext,\n    params: Vec&lt;Expr&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n    desc: &amp;StatementDesc,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Params, PlanError&gt; {\n    if params.len() != desc.param_types.len() {\n        sql_bail!(\n            \"expected {} params, got {}\",\n            desc.param_types.len(),\n            params.len()\n        );\n    }\n\n    let qcx = QueryContext::root(scx, QueryLifetime::OneShot);\n\n    let mut datums = Row::default();\n    let mut packer = datums.packer();\n    let mut actual_types = Vec::new();\n    let temp_storage = &amp;RowArena::new();\n    for (i, (mut expr, expected_ty)) in params.into_iter().zip_eq(&amp;desc.param_types).enumerate() {\n        transform_ast::transform(scx, &amp;mut expr)?;\n\n        let ecx = execute_expr_context(&amp;qcx);\n        let ex = plan_expr(&amp;ecx, &amp;expr)?.type_as_any(&amp;ecx)?;\n        let actual_ty = ecx.scalar_type(&amp;ex);\n        if plan_hypothetical_cast(&amp;ecx, *EXECUTE_CAST_CONTEXT, &amp;actual_ty, expected_ty).is_none() {\n            return Err(PlanError::WrongParameterType(\n                i + 1,\n                ecx.humanize_scalar_type(expected_ty, false),\n                ecx.humanize_scalar_type(&amp;actual_ty, false),\n            ));\n        }\n        let ex = ex.lower_uncorrelated(scx.catalog.system_vars())?;\n        let evaled = ex.eval(&amp;[], temp_storage)?;\n        packer.push(evaled);\n        actual_types.push(actual_ty);\n    }\n    Ok(Params {\n        datums,\n        execute_types: actual_types,\n        expected_types: desc.param_types.clone(),\n    })\n}\n\nstatic EXECUTE_CONTEXT_SCOPE: LazyLock&lt;Scope&gt; = LazyLock::new(Scope::empty);\nstatic EXECUTE_CONTEXT_REL_TYPE: LazyLock&lt;SqlRelationType&gt; = LazyLock::new(SqlRelationType::empty);\n\n/// Returns an &#96;ExprContext&#96; for the expressions in the parameters of an EXECUTE statement.\npub(crate) fn execute_expr_context&lt;'a&gt;(qcx: &amp;'a QueryContext&lt;'a&gt;) -&gt; ExprContext&lt;'a&gt; {\n    ExprContext {\n        qcx,\n        name: \"EXECUTE\",",

        "contents": "/// planning), and then recovering them after the join (if needed) by duplicating existing columns.\n///\n/// After the last run of &#96;Demand&#96;, there should always be a &#96;ProjectionPushdown&#96;, so that dummies\n/// are eliminated from plans.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct Demand {\n    recursion_guard: RecursionGuard,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Demand {\n    fn default() -&gt; Demand {\n        Demand {\n            recursion_guard: RecursionGuard::with_limit(RECURSION_LIMIT),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl CheckedRecursion for Demand {\n    fn recursion_guard(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;RecursionGuard {\n        &amp;self.recursion_guard\n    }\n}\n\nimpl crate::Transform for Demand {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        \"Demand\"\n    }\n\n    #[mz_ore::instrument(\n        target = \"optimizer\",\n        level = \"debug\",\n        fields(path.segment = \"demand\")\n    )]\n    fn actually_perform_transform(\n        &amp;self,\n        relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr,\n        _: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), crate::TransformError&gt; {\n        let result = self.action(\n            relation,\n            (0..relation.arity()).collect(),\n            &amp;mut BTreeMap::new(),\n        );\n        mz_repr::explain::trace_plan(&amp;*relation);\n        result\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Demand {\n    /// Columns to be produced.",

        "contents": "(path.segment =\"global\")\n)]\npub fn optimize_dataflow(\n    dataflow: &amp;mut DataflowDesc,\n    transform_ctx: &amp;mut TransformCtx,\n    fast_path_optimizer: bool,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(), TransformError&gt; {\n    // Inline views that are used in only one other view.\n    inline_views(dataflow)?;\n\n    if fast_path_optimizer {\n        optimize_dataflow_relations(\n            dataflow,\n            &amp;Optimizer::fast_path_optimizer(transform_ctx),\n            transform_ctx,\n        )?;\n    } else {\n        // Logical optimization pass after view inlining\n        optimize_dataflow_relations(\n            dataflow,\n            #[allow(deprecated)]\n            &amp;Optimizer::logical_optimizer(transform_ctx),\n            transform_ctx,\n        )?;\n\n        optimize_dataflow_filters(dataflow)?;\n        // TODO: when the linear operator contract ensures that propagated\n        // predicates are always applied, projections and filters can be removed\n        // from where they come from. Once projections and filters can be removed,\n        // TODO: it would be useful for demand to be optimized after filters\n        // that way demand only includes the columns that are still necessary after\n        // the filters are applied.\n        optimize_dataflow_demand(dataflow)?;\n\n        // A smaller logical optimization pass after projections and filters are\n        // pushed down across views.\n        optimize_dataflow_relations(\n            dataflow,\n            &amp;Optimizer::logical_cleanup_pass(transform_ctx, false),\n            transform_ctx,\n        )?;\n\n        // Physical optimization pass\n        optimize_dataflow_relations(\n            dataflow,\n            &amp;Optimizer::physical_optimizer(transform_ctx),\n            transform_ctx,\n        )?;\n\n        optimize_dataflow_monotonic(dataflow, transform_ctx)?;\n    }\n\n    prune_and_annotate_dataflow_index_imports(\n        dataflow,\n        transform_ctx.indexes,\n        transform_ctx.df_meta,\n    )?;\n\n    // Warning: If you want to add a transform call here, consider it very carefully whether it",

        "contents": "                    if non_literal_keys != group_key.len() || non_constant_aggr != aggregates.len()\n                    {\n                        let first_projected_literal: usize = non_literal_keys + non_constant_aggr;\n                        let mut projection = Vec::new();\n                        let mut projected_literals = Vec::new();\n\n                        let mut new_group_key = Vec::new();\n                        for key in group_key.drain(..) {\n                            if key.is_literal() {\n                                projection.push(first_projected_literal + projected_literals.len());\n                                projected_literals.push(key);\n                            } else {\n                                projection.push(new_group_key.len());\n                                new_group_key.push(key);\n                            }\n                        }\n                        // The new group key without literals\n                        *group_key = new_group_key;\n\n                        let mut new_aggregates = Vec::new();\n                        for aggr in aggregates.drain(..) {\n                            if aggr.is_constant() {\n                                projection.push(first_projected_literal + projected_literals.len());\n                                projected_literals.push(eval_constant_aggr(&amp;aggr));\n                            } else {\n                                projection.push(group_key.len() + new_aggregates.len());\n *aggregates = new_aggregates;\n\n                        *relation = relation\n                            .take_dangerous()\n                            .map(projected_literals)\n                            .project(projection);\n                    }\n                    Ok(result)\n                }\n                MirRelationExpr::TopK {\n                    input,",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "                        if input_literals.iter().any(|l| !l.is_empty()) {\n                            *implementation = mz_expr::JoinImplementation::Unimplemented;\n\n                            // We should be able to install any literals in the\n                            // equivalence relations, and then lift all literals\n                            // around the join using a project to re-order columns.\n\n                            // Visit each expression in each equivalence class to either\n                            // inline literals or update column references.\n                            let new_input_mapper = JoinInputMapper::new(inputs);\n                            for equivalence in equivalences.iter_mut() {\n                                for expr in equivalence.iter_mut() {\n                                    expr.visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n                                        if let MirScalarExpr::Column(c, _) = e {\n                                            let (col, input) =\n                                                old_input_mapper.map_column_to_local(*c);\n                                            if col &gt;= new_input_mapper.input_arity(input) {\n                                                // the column refers to a literal that\n                                                // has been promoted. inline it\n                                                *e = input_literals[input]\n                                                    [col - new_input_mapper.input_arity(input)]\n                                                .clone()\n                                            } else {\n                                                // localize to the new join\n                                                *c = new_input_mapper\n                                                    .map_column_to_global(col, input);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n                                    })?;\n                                }\n                            }",

        "contents": "    Ok(expr)\n}\n\nfn plan_literal&lt;'a&gt;(l: &amp;'a Value) -&gt; Result&lt;CoercibleScalarExpr, PlanError&gt; {\n    let (datum, scalar_type) = match l {\n        Value::Number(s) =&gt; {\n            let d = strconv::parse_numeric(s.as_str())?;\n            if !s.contains(&amp;['E', '.'][..]) {\n                // Maybe representable as an int?\n                if let Ok(n) = d.0.try_into() {\n                    (Datum::Int32(n), SqlScalarType::Int32)\n                } else if let Ok(n) = d.0.try_into() {\n                    (Datum::Int64(n), SqlScalarType::Int64)\n                } else {\n                    (\n                        Datum::Numeric(d),\n                        SqlScalarType::Numeric { max_scale: None },\n                    )\n                }\n            } else {\n                (\n                    Datum::Numeric(d),\n                    SqlScalarType::Numeric { max_scale: None },\n                )\n            }\n        }\n        Value::HexString(_) =&gt; bail_unsupported!(\"hex string literals\"),\n        Value::Boolean(b) =&gt; match b {\n            false =&gt; (Datum::False, SqlScalarType::Bool),\n            true =&gt; (Datum::True, SqlScalarType::Bool),\n        },\n        Value::Interval(i) =&gt; {\n            let i = literal::plan_interval(i)?;\n            (Datum::Interval(i), SqlScalarType::Interval)\n        }\n        Value::String(s) =&gt; return Ok(CoercibleScalarExpr::LiteralString(s.clone())),\n        Value::Null =&gt; return Ok(CoercibleScalarExpr::LiteralNull),\n    };\n    let expr = HirScalarExpr::literal(datum, scalar_type);\n    Ok(expr.into())\n}\n\n/// The common part of the planning of non-aggregate window functions, i.e.,\n/// scalar window functions and value window functions.\nfn plan_window_function_non_aggr&lt;'a&gt;(\n    ecx: &amp;ExprContext,\n    Function {\n        name,\n        args,\n        filter,\n        over,",

plan_field_access(ecx, expr, field),\n        Expr::WildcardAccess(expr) =&gt; plan_expr(ecx, expr),\n        Expr::Subscript { expr, positions } =&gt; plan_subscript(ecx, expr, positions),\n        Expr::Like {\n            expr,\n            pattern,\n            escape,\n            case_insensitive,\n            negated,\n        } =&gt;       "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/explain.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 84,

        },
        "contents": "    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; HirRelationExpr {\n    fn as_explain_single_plan(\n        &amp;'a mut self,\n        context: &amp;'a ExplainContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;ExplainSinglePlan&lt;'a, HirRelationExpr&gt;, ExplainError&gt; {\n        // unless raw plans are explicitly requested\n        // ensure that all nested subqueries are wrapped in Let blocks by calling\n        // &#96;normalize_subqueries&#96;\n        if !context.config.raw_plans {\n            mz_ore::panic::catch_unwind_str(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {\n                normalize_subqueries(self).map_err(|e| e.into())\n            }))\n            .unwrap_or_else(|panic| {\n                // A panic during optimization is always a bug; log an error so we learn about it.\n                // TODO(teskje): collect and log a backtrace from the panic site\n                tracing::error!(\"caught a panic during &#96;normalize_subqueries&#96;: {panic}\");\n\n                let msg = format!(\"unexpected panic during &#96;normalize_subqueries&#96;: {panic}\");\n                Err(ExplainError::UnknownError(msg))\n            })?\n        }\n\n        // TODO: use config values to infer requested\n        // plan annotations\n        let plan = AnnotatedPlan {\n            plan: self,\n            annotations: Default::default(),\n        };\n        Ok(ExplainSinglePlan { context, plan })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Normalize the way subqueries appear in [&#96;HirScalarExpr::Exists&#96;]\n/// or [&#96;HirScalarExpr::Select&#96;] variants.\n///\n/// After the transform is applied, subqueries are pulled as a value in\n/// a let binding enclosing the [&#96;HirRelationExpr&#96;] parent of the\n/// [&#96;HirScalarExpr::Exists&#96;] or [&#96;HirScalarExpr::Select&#96;] where the\n/// subquery appears, and the corresponding variant references the\n/// new binding with a [&#96;HirRelationExpr::Get&#96;].\npub fn normalize_subqueries&lt;'a&gt;(expr: &amp;'a mut HirRelationExpr) -&gt; Result&lt;(), RecursionLimitError&gt; {\n    // A helper struct to represent accumulated &#96;$local_id = $subquery&#96;\n    // bindings that need to be installed in &#96;let ... in $expr&#96; nodes",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/transform/src/canonicalization/flat_map_elimination.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 47,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 71,
            "column": 23
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl FlatMapElimination {\n    /// Apply &#96;FlatMapElimination&#96; to the root of the given &#96;MirRelationExpr&#96;.\n    pub fn action(relation: &amp;mut MirRelationExpr) {\n        // Treat Wrap specially: we can sometimes optimize it out even when it has non-literal\n        // arguments.\n        //\n        // (No need to look for WithOrdinality here, as that never occurs with Wrap: users can't\n        // call Wrap directly; we only create calls to Wrap ourselves, and we don't use\n        // WithOrdinality on it.)\n        if let MirRelationExpr::FlatMap { func, exprs, input } = relation {\n            if let TableFunc::Wrap { width, .. } = func {\n                if *width &gt;= exprs.len() {\n                    *relation = input.take_dangerous().map(std::mem::take(exprs));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        // For all other table functions (and Wraps that are not covered by the above), check\n        // whether all arguments are literals (with no errors), in which case we'll evaluate the\n        // table function and check how many output rows it has, and maybe turn the FlatMap into\n        // something simpler.\n        if let MirRelationExpr::FlatMap { func, exprs, input } = relation {\n            if let Some(args) = exprs\n                .iter()",

        },
        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Renders a plan into a timely/differential dataflow computation.\n//!\n//! ## Error handling\n//!\n//! Timely and differential have no idioms for computations that can error. The\n//! philosophy is, reasonably, to define the semantics of the computation such\n//! that errors are unnecessary: e.g., by using wrap-around semantics for\n//! integer overflow.\n//!\n//! Unfortunately, SQL semantics are not nearly so elegant, and require errors\n//! in myriad cases. The classic example is a division by zero, but invalid\n//! input for casts, overflowing integer operations, and dozens of other\n//! functions need the ability to produce errors ar runtime.\n//!\n//! At the moment, only *scalar* expression evaluation can fail, so only\n//! operators that evaluate scalar expressions can fail. At the time of writing,\n//! that includes map, filter, reduce, and join operators. Constants are a bit\n//! of a special case: they can be either a constant vector of rows *or* a\n//! constant, singular error.\n//!\n//! The approach taken is to build two parallel trees of computation: one for\n//! the rows that have been successfully evaluated (the \"oks tree\"), and one for\n//! the errors that have been generated (the \"errs tree\"). For example:\n//!\n//! &#96;&#96;&#96;text\n//!    oks1  errs1       oks2  errs2\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!   project  |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!     map    |           |     |\n//!      |\\    |           |     |\n//!      | \\   |           |     |\n//!      |  \\  |           |     |\n//!      |   \\ |           |     |\n//!      |    \\|           |     |\n//!   project  +           +     +",

            "line": 780,

          }
        },
        "contents": "        let (stream, errors) = self.flat_map(key_val, max_demand, move |row_datums, time, diff| {\n            let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n            let until = std::rc::Rc::clone(&amp;until);\n            let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n            let row_iter = row_datums.iter();\n            let mut datums_local = datum_vec.borrow();\n            datums_local.extend(row_iter);\n            let time = time.clone();\n            let event_time = time.event_time();\n            mfp_plan\n                .evaluate(\n                    &amp;mut datums_local,\n                    &amp;temp_storage,\n                    event_time,\n                    diff.clone(),\n                    move |time| !until.less_equal(time),\n                    &amp;mut row_builder,\n                )\n                .map(move |x| match x {\n                    Ok((row, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                        // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                        let mut time: S::Timestamp = time.clone();\n                        *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                        (Ok(row), time, diff)\n                    }\n                    Err((e, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                        // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                        let mut time: S::Timestamp = time.clone();\n                        *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                        (Err(e), time, diff)\n                    }\n                })\n        });\n\n        use differential_dataflow::AsCollection;\n        let (oks, errs) = stream\n            .as_collection()\n            .map_fallible::&lt;CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;_&gt;, CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;_&gt;, _, _, _&gt;(\n                \"OkErr\",\n                |x| x,\n            );\n\n        (oks, errors.concat(&amp;errs))\n    }\n    pub fn ensure_collections(\n        mut self,",

        "contents": "        'collect: while limit_remaining &gt; 0 {\n            let Some(batch) = cursor.next().await else {\n                break;\n            };\n            for (data, _, d) in batch {\n                let row = data.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n\n                if let Some(literal) = &amp;literal_constraint {\n                    match row.iter().take(literal_len).cmp(literal.iter()) {\n                        Ordering::Less =&gt; continue,\n                        Ordering::Equal =&gt; {}\n                        Ordering::Greater =&gt; break 'collect,\n                    }\n                }\n\n                let count: usize = d.try_into().map_err(|_| {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        shard = %metadata.data_shard, diff = d, ?row,\n                        \"persist peek encountered negative multiplicities\",\n                    );\n                    format!(\n                        \"Invalid data in source, \\\n                         saw retractions ({}) for row that does not exist: {:?}\",\n                        -d, row,\n                    )\n                })?;\n                let Some(count) = NonZeroUsize::new(count) else {\n                    continue;\n                };\n                let mut datum_local = datum_vec.borrow_with(&amp;row);\n                let eval_result = mfp_plan\n                    .evaluate_into(&amp;mut datum_local, &amp;arena, &amp;mut row_builder)\n                    .map(|row| row.cloned())\n                    .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n                if let Some(row) = eval_result {\n                    total_size = total_size\n                        .",

        "contents": "        let (oks, errs) = stream.unary_fallible(Pipeline, \"FlatMapStage\", move |_, info| {\n            let activator = scope.activator_for(info.address);\n            Box::new(move |input, ok_output, err_output| {\n                let mut datums = DatumVec::new();\n                let mut datums_mfp = DatumVec::new();\n\n                // Buffer for extensions to &#96;input_row&#96;.\n                let mut table_func_output = Vec::new();\n\n                let mut budget = budget;\n\n                while let Some((cap, data)) = input.next() {\n                    let mut ok_session = ok_output.session_with_builder(&amp;cap);\n                    let mut err_session = err_output.session_with_builder(&amp;cap);\n\n                    'input: for (input_row, time, diff) in data.drain(..) {\n                        let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n\n                        // Unpack datums for expression evaluation.\n                        let datums_local = datums.borrow_with(&amp;input_row);\n                        let args = exprs\n                            .iter()\n                            .map(|e| e.eval(&amp;datums_local, &amp;temp_storage))\n                            .collect::&lt;Result&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;, _&gt;&gt;();\n                        let args = match args {\n                            Ok(args) =&gt; args,\n                            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                                err_session.give((e.into(), time, diff));\n                                continue 'input;\n                            }\n                        };\n                        let mut extensions = match func.eval(&amp;args, &amp;temp_storage) {\n                            Ok(exts) =&gt; exts.fuse(),\n                            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                                err_session.give((e.into(), time, diff));\n                                continue 'input;\n                            }\n                        };\n\n                        // Draw additional columns out of the table func evaluation.\n                        while let Some((extension, output_diff)) = extensions.next() {\n                            table_func_output.push((extension, output_diff));",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

            "column": 14

        "contents": "                        }\n                        match (&amp;aggr.func, &amp;accum) {\n                            (AggregateFunc::SumUInt16, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. })\n                            | (AggregateFunc::SumUInt32, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. })\n                            | (AggregateFunc::SumUInt64, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. }) =&gt; {\n                                if accum.is_negative() {\n                                    error_logger.log(\n                                    \"Invalid negative unsigned aggregation in ReduceAccumulable\",\n                                    &amp;format!(\"aggr={aggr:?}, accum={accum:?}\"),\n                                );\n                                    let key = key.to_row();\n                                    let message = format!(\n                                        \"Invalid data in source, saw negative accumulation with \\\n                                         unsigned type for key {key}\"\n                                    );\n                                    output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                                }\n                            }\n                            _ =&gt; (), // no more errors to check for at this point!\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // If &#96;mfp_after&#96; can error, then evaluate it here.\n                    let Some(mfp) = &amp;mfp_after2 else { return };\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                    let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                    let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                    datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n                    for (aggr, accum) in full_aggrs2.iter().zip_eq(accums) {\n                        datums_local.push(finalize_accum(&amp;aggr.func, accum, total));\n                    }\n\n                    if let Result::Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage) {\n                        output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n                },\n            );",

        "contents": "(demand);\n\n            let (key_val_input, err_input) = input.enter_region(inner).flat_map(\n                input_key.map(|k| (k, None)),\n                max_demand,\n                move |row_datums, time, diff| {\n                    let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n\n                    let mut row_iter = row_datums.drain(..);\n                    let mut datums_local = datums.borrow();\n                    // Unpack only the demanded columns.\n                    for skip in skips.iter() {\n                        datums_local.push(row_iter.nth(*skip).unwrap());\n                    }\n\n                    // Evaluate the key expressions.\n                    let key =\n                        key_plan.evaluate_into(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage, &amp;mut row_builder);\n                    let key = match key {\n                        Err(e) =&gt; {\n                            return Some((Err(DataflowError::from(e)), time.clone(), diff.clone()));\n                        }\n                        Ok(Some(key)) =&gt; key.clone(),\n                        Ok(None) =&gt; panic!(\"Row expected as no predicate was used\"),\n                    };\n\n                    // Evaluate the value expressions.\n                    // The prior evaluation may have left additional columns we should delete.\n                    datums_local.truncate(skips.len());\n                    let val =\n                        val_plan.evaluate_into(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage, &amp;mut row_builder);\n                    let val = match val {\n                        Err(e) =&gt; {\n                            return Some((Err(DataflowError::from(e)), time.clone(), diff.clone()));\n                        }\n                        Ok(Some(val)) =&gt; val.clone(),\n                        Ok(None) =&gt; panic!(\"Row expected as no predicate was used\"),\n                    };\n\n                    Some((Ok((key, val)), time.clone(), diff.clone()))\n                },\n            );\n\n            //",

        },
        "contents": "(\"Reduced TopK input\", {\n        move |mut hash_key, source, target: &amp;mut Vec&lt;(Tr::ValOwn, Diff)&gt;| {\n            // Unpack the limit, either into an integer literal or an expression to evaluate.\n            let limit = match &amp;limit {\n                Some(Ok(lit)) =&gt; Some(*lit),\n                Some(Err(expr)) =&gt; {\n                    // Unpack &#96;key&#96; after skipping the hash and determine the limit.\n                    // If the limit errors, use a zero limit; errors are surfaced elsewhere.\n                    let temp_storage = mz_repr::RowArena::new();\n                    let _hash = hash_key.next();\n                    let mut key_datums = datum_vec.borrow();\n                    key_datums.extend(hash_key);\n                    let datum_limit = expr\n                        .eval(&amp;key_datums, &amp;temp_storage)\n                        .unwrap_or(Datum::Int64(0));\n                    Some(match datum_limit {\n                        Datum::Null =&gt; Diff::MAX,\n                        d =&gt; Diff::from(d.unwrap_int64()),\n                    })\n                }\n                None =&gt; None,\n            };\n\n            if let Some(err) = Tr::ValOwn::into_error() {\n                for (datums, diff) in source.iter() {\n                    if diff.is_positive() {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    target.push((err((*datums).to_row()), Diff::ONE));\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Determine if we must actually shrink the result set.\n            let must_shrink = offset &gt; 0\n                || limit\n                    .map(|l| source.iter().map(|(_, d)| *d).sum::&lt;Diff&gt;() &gt; l)\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n            if !must_shrink {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // First go ahead and emit all records. Note that we ensure target\n            // has the capacity to hold at least these records, and avoid any\n            // dependencies on the user-provided (potentially unbounded) limit.\n            target.reserve(source.len());\n            for (datums, diff) in source.iter()",

        },
        "contents": "        let (input, oks, errs) = if validating {\n            // Build topk stage, produce errors for invalid multiplicities.\n            let (input, stage) = build_topk_negated_stage::&lt;\n                S,\n                RowValBuilder&lt;_, _, _&gt;,\n                RowValSpine&lt;Result&lt;Row, Row&gt;, _, _&gt;,\n            &gt;(&amp;input, order_key, offset, limit, arity);\n            let stage = stage.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.clone()));\n\n            // Demux oks and errors.\n            let error_logger = self.error_logger();\n            type CB&lt;C&gt; = CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;C&gt;;\n            let (oks, errs) = stage.map_fallible::&lt;CB&lt;_&gt;, CB&lt;_&gt;, _, _, _&gt;(\n                \"Demuxing Errors\",\n                move |(hk, result)| match result {\n                    Err(v) =&gt; {\n                        let mut hk_iter = hk.iter();\n                        let h = hk_iter.next().unwrap().unwrap_uint64();\n                        let k = SharedRow::pack(hk_iter);\n                        let message = \"Negative multiplicities in TopK\";\n                        error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"k={k:?}, h={h}, v={v:?}\"));\n                        Err(EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into())\n                    }\n                    Ok(t) =&gt; Ok((hk, t)),\n                },\n            );\n            (input, oks, Some(errs))\n        } else {\n            // Build non-validating topk stage.\n            let (input, stage) =\n                build_topk_negated_stage::&lt;S, RowRowBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                    &amp;input, order_key, offset, limit, arity,\n                );\n            // Turn arrangement into collection.\n            let stage = stage.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.to_row()));\n\n            (input, stage, None)\n        };\n        let input = input.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.to_row()));\n        (oks.concat(&amp;input), errs)\n    }\n\n    fn render_top1_monotonic&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1146
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1191,
            "column": 32
          }
        },
        "contents": "    fn render_plan_expr(\n        &amp;mut self,\n        expr: render_plan::Expr,\n        collections: &amp;BTreeMap&lt;LirId, CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt;&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt; {\n        use render_plan::Expr::*;\n\n        let expect_input = |id| {\n            collections\n                .get(&amp;id)\n                .cloned()\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(\"missing input collection: {id}\"))\n        };\n\n        match expr {\n            Constant { rows } =&gt; {\n                // Produce both rows and errs to avoid conditional dataflow construction.\n                let (rows, errs) = match rows {\n                    Ok(rows) =&gt; (rows, Vec::new()),\n                    Err(e) =&gt; (Vec::new(), vec![e]),\n                };\n\n                // We should advance times in constant collections to start from &#96;as_of&#96;.\n                let as_of_frontier = self.as_of_frontier.clone();\n                let until = self.until.clone();\n                let ok_collection = rows\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .filter_map(move |(row, mut time, diff)| {\n                        time.advance_by(as_of_frontier.borrow());\n                        if !until.less_equal(&amp;time) {\n                            Some((\n                                row,\n                                &lt;G::Timestamp as Refines&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;&gt;::to_inner(time),\n                                diff,\n                            ))\n                        } else {\n                            None\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .to_stream(&amp;mut self.scope)\n                    .as_collection();\n\n                let mut error_time: mz_repr::Timestamp = Timestamp::minimum();\n                error_time.advance_by(self.as_of_frontier.borrow());\n                let err_collection = errs\n                    .into_iter()",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    fn render_plan_expr(",
            "lineNumber": 1147
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;mut self,",
            "lineNumber": 1148
          },
          {
            "text": "        expr: render_plan::Expr,",
            "lineNumber": 1149
          },
          {
            "text": "        collections: &amp;BTreeMap&lt;LirId, CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt;&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 1150
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 1151
          },

        "contents": "                            for datum in func2\n                                .eval_with_unnest_list::&lt;_, window_agg_helpers::OneByOneAggrImpls&gt;(\n                                    iter,\n                                    &amp;temp_storage,\n                                )\n                            {\n                                datums_local.truncate(key_len);\n                                datums_local.push(datum);\n                                // We know that &#96;mfp&#96; can error (because of the &#96;could_error&#96; call\n                                // above), so try to evaluate it here.\n                                if let Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage)\n                                {\n                                    target.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                                }\n                            }\n                        },\n                    )\n                    .as_collection(|_, v| v.clone())\n            };\n\n            if let Some(e) = err_output {\n                err_output = Some(e.concat(&amp;errs));\n            } else {\n                err_output = Some(errs);\n            }\n        }\n        (oks, err_output)\n    }\n\n    fn build_reduce_inaccumulable_distinct",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

          "startPosition": {
            "line": 45
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 91
          }
        },
        "contents": "//!      |    \\|           |     |\n//!   project  +           +     +\n//!      |     |          /     /\n//!      |     |         /     /\n//!    join ------------+     /\n//!      |     |             /\n//!      |     | +----------+\n//!      |     |/\n//!     oks   errs\n//! &#96;&#96;&#96;\n//!\n//! The project operation cannot fail, so errors from errs1 are propagated\n//! directly. Map operators are fallible and so can inject additional errors\n//! into the stream. Join operators combine the errors from each of their\n//! inputs.\n//!\n//! The semantics of the error stream are minimal. From the perspective of SQL,\n//! a dataflow is considered to be in an error state if there is at least one\n//! element in the final errs collection. The error value returned to the user\n//! is selected arbitrarily; SQL only makes provisions to return one error to\n//! the user at a time. There are plans to make the err collection accessible to\n//! end users, so they can see all errors at once.\n//!\n//! To make errors transient, simply ensure that the operator can retract any\n//! produced errors when corrected data arrives. To make errors permanent, write\n//! the operator such that it never retracts the errors it produced. Future work\n//! will likely want to introduce some sort of sort order for errors, so that\n//! permanent errors are returned to the user ahead of transient errors—probably\n//! by introducing a new error type a la:\n//!\n//! &#96;&#96;&#96;no_run\n//! # struct EvalError;\n//! # struct SourceError;\n//! enum DataflowError {\n//!     Transient(EvalError),\n//!     Permanent(SourceError),\n//! }\n//! &#96;&#96;&#96;\n//!\n//! If the error stream is empty, the oks stream must be correct. If the error\n//! stream is non-empty, then there are no semantics for the oks stream. This is\n//! sufficient to support SQL in its current form, but is likely to be\n//! unsatisfactory long term. We suspect that we can continue to imbue the oks\n//! stream with semantics if we are very careful in describing what data should\n//! and should not be produced upon encountering an error. Roughly speaking, the\n//! oks stream could represent the correct result of the computation where all",

        "contents": ",\n}\n\nimpl ExprPrep for ExprPrepOneShot&lt;'_&gt; {\n    fn prep_relation_expr(\n        &amp;self,\n        expr: &amp;mut OptimizedMirRelationExpr,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        expr.0\n            .try_visit_scalars_mut(&amp;mut |s| self.prep_scalar_expr(s))\n    }\n\n    fn prep_scalar_expr(&amp;self, expr: &amp;mut MirScalarExpr) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        // Evaluate each unmaterializable function and replace the\n        // invocation with the result.\n        expr.try_visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n            if let MirScalarExpr::CallUnmaterializable(f) = e {\n                *e = eval_unmaterializable_func(\n                    self.catalog_state,\n                    f,\n                    self.logical_time,\n                    self.session,\n                )?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Prepare an expression for evaluation in a CHECK expression of a webhook source.\n/// Replaces calls to &#96;UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp&#96;, others are left untouched.\npub struct ExprPrepWebhookValidation {\n    /// Time at which this expression is being evaluated.\n    pub now: DateTime&lt;Utc&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl ExprPrep for ExprPrepWebhookValidation {\n    fn prep_relation_expr(\n        &amp;self,\n        expr: &amp;mut OptimizedMirRelationExpr,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        expr.0\n            .try_visit_scalars_mut(&amp;mut |s| self.prep_scalar_expr(s))\n    }\n\n    fn prep_scalar_expr(&amp;self, expr: &amp;mut MirScalarExpr) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        let now = self.now;\n        expr.try_visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n            if let MirScalarExpr::CallUnmaterializable(f @ UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp) =\n                e\n            {\n                let now: Datum = now.try_into()?;\n                let const_expr = MirScalarExpr::literal_ok(now, f.output_type().scalar_type);\n                *e = const_expr;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\npub enum EvalTime {\n    Time(mz_repr::Timestamp),\n    /// Skips mz_now() calls.\n    Deferred,",

        },
        "contents": "    for (cols, diff) in extensions.iter() {\n        // Arrange &#96;datums_local&#96; to reflect the intended output pre-mfp.\n        datums_local.truncate(datums_len);\n        datums_local.extend(cols.iter());\n\n        let results = mfp_plan.evaluate(\n            &amp;mut datums_local,\n            &amp;temp_storage,\n            event_time,\n            *diff * *input_diff,\n            |time| !until.less_equal(time),\n            &amp;mut row_builder,\n        );\n\n        for result in results {\n            *budget = budget.saturating_sub(1);\n            match result {\n                Ok((row, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                    // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                    let mut time = input_time.clone();\n                    *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                    ok_output.give((row, time, diff));\n                }\n                Err((err, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                    // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                    let mut time = input_time.clone();\n                    *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                    err_output.give((err, time, diff));\n                }\n            };\n        }\n    }\n}",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "            // (database-issues#7108).\n\n            match top_k_plan.limit().map(|l| (l.as_literal(), l)) {\n                None =&gt; {}\n                Some((Some(Ok(literal)), _))\n                    if literal == Datum::Null || literal.unwrap_int64() &gt;= 0 =&gt; {}\n                Some((_, expr)) =&gt; {\n                    // Produce errors from limit selectors that error or are\n                    // negative, and nothing from limit selectors that do\n                    // not. Note that even if expr.could_error() is false,\n                    // the expression might still return a negative limit and\n                    // thus needs to be checked.\n                    let expr = expr.clone();\n                    let mut datum_vec = mz_repr::DatumVec::new();\n                    let errors = ok_input.flat_map(move |row| {\n                        let temp_storage = mz_repr::RowArena::new();\n                        let datums = datum_vec.borrow_with(&amp;row);\n                        match expr.eval(&amp;datums[..], &amp;temp_storage) {\n                            Ok(l) if l != Datum::Null &amp;&amp; l.unwrap_int64() &lt; 0 =&gt; {\n                                Some(EvalError::NegLimit.into())\n                            }\n                            Ok(_) =&gt; None,\n                            Err(e) =&gt; Some(e.into()),\n                        }\n                    });\n                    err_collection = err_collection.concat(&amp;errors);\n                }\n            }\n\n            let ok_result =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/optimize/dataflows.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 155
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 206,
            "column": 37
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub struct ExprPrepMaintained;\n\nimpl ExprPrep for ExprPrepMaintained {\n    fn prep_relation_expr(\n        &amp;self,\n        expr: &amp;mut OptimizedMirRelationExpr,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        expr.0.try_visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n            // Carefully test filter expressions, which may represent temporal filters.\n            if let MirRelationExpr::Filter { input, predicates } = &amp;*e {\n                let mfp = MapFilterProject::new(input.arity()).filter(predicates.iter().cloned());\n                match mfp.into_plan() {\n                    Err(e) =&gt; Err(OptimizerError::UnsupportedTemporalExpression(e)),\n                    Ok(mut mfp) =&gt; {\n                        for s in mfp.iter_nontemporal_exprs() {\n                            self.prep_scalar_expr(s)?;\n                        }\n                        Ok(())\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                e.try_visit_scalars_mut1(&amp;mut |s| self.prep_scalar_expr(s))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn prep_scalar_expr(&amp;self, expr: &amp;mut MirScalarExpr) -&gt; Result&lt;(), OptimizerError&gt; {\n        // Reject the query if it contains any unmaterializable function calls.\n        let mut last_observed_unmaterializable_func = None;\n        expr.visit_mut_post(&amp;mut |e| {\n            if let MirScalarExpr::CallUnmaterializable(f) = e {\n                last_observed_unmaterializable_func = Some(f.clone());\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(f) = last_observed_unmaterializable_func {\n            Err(OptimizerError::UnmaterializableFunction(f))\n        } else {\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Prepare an expression to run once at a logical time in a session.\n/// Calls to all unmaterializable functions are replaced with constants.\npub struct ExprPrepOneShot&lt;'a&gt; {\n    pub logical_time: EvalTime,\n    pub session: &amp;'a dyn SessionMetadata,\n    pub catalog_state: &amp;'a CatalogState,\n}\n\nimpl ExprPrep for ExprPrepOneShot&lt;'_&gt;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/compute_state.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1448
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1503,
            "column": 29
          }
        },
        "contents": "    fn collect_finished_data(\n        &amp;mut self,\n        max_result_size: u64,\n        peek_stash_eligible: bool,\n        peek_stash_threshold_bytes: usize,\n        metrics: &amp;IndexPeekMetrics&lt;'_&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; PeekStatus {\n        let error_scan_start = Instant::now();\n\n        // Check if there exist any errors and, if so, return whatever one we\n        // find first.\n        let (mut cursor, storage) = self.trace_bundle.errs_mut().cursor();\n        while cursor.key_valid(&amp;storage) {\n            let mut copies = Diff::ZERO;\n            cursor.map_times(&amp;storage, |time, diff| {\n                if time.less_equal(&amp;self.peek.timestamp) {\n                    copies += diff;\n                }\n            });\n            if copies.is_negative() {\n                let error = cursor.key(&amp;storage);\n                tracing::error!(\n                    target = %self.peek.target.id(), diff = %copies, %error,\n                    \"index peek encountered negative multiplicities in error trace\",\n                );\n                return PeekStatus::Ready(PeekResponse::Error(format!(\n                    \"Invalid data in source errors, \\\n                    saw retractions ({}) for row that does not exist: {}\",\n                    -copies, error,\n                )));\n            }\n            if copies.is_positive() {\n                return PeekStatus::Ready(PeekResponse::Error(cursor.key(&amp;storage).to_string()));\n            }\n            cursor.step_key(&amp;storage);\n        }\n\n        metrics\n            .error_scan_seconds\n            .observe(error_scan_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64());\n\n        Self::collect_ok_finished_data(\n            &amp;self.peek,\n            self.trace_bundle.oks_mut(),\n            max_result_size,\n            peek_stash_eligible,\n            peek_stash_threshold_bytes,\n            metrics,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Collects data for a known-complete peek from the ok stream.\n    fn collect_ok_finished_data&lt;Tr&gt;(\n        peek: &amp;Peek&lt;Timestamp&gt;,\n        oks_handle: &amp;mut Tr,\n        max_result_size: u64,",

          {
            "text": "    /// Collects data for a known-complete peek from the ok stream.",

            "text": "    fn collect_ok_finished_data&lt;Tr&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 1501
          },
          {
            "text": "        peek: &amp;Peek&lt;Timestamp&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 1502

            "text": "        oks_handle: &amp;mut Tr,",

      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 1465,
            "column": 55
          }
        },
        "contents": "        // to scan for these potential errors. Note that we cannot directly use\n        // &#96;mz_timely_util::reduce::ReduceExt::reduce_pair&#96; here because we only\n        // conditionally render the second component of the reduction pair.\n        if let Some(mfp) = mfp_after2 {\n            let mfp_errs = arranged\n                .mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, RowErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowErrSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                    \"ReduceMonotonic Error Check\",\n                    move |key, input, output| {\n                        let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                        let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                        let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                        datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n                        let accum = &amp;input[0].1;\n                        for monoid in accum.iter() {\n                            datums_local.extend(monoid.finalize().iter());\n                        }\n                        if let Result::Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage)\n                        {\n                            output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                        }\n                    },\n                )\n                .as_collection(|_k, v| v.clone());\n            (output, validation_errs.concat(&amp;mfp_errs))\n        } else {\n            (output, validation_errs)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the dataflow to compute and arrange multiple accumulable aggregations.\n    ///\n    /// The incoming values are moved to the update's \"difference\" field, at which point\n    /// they can be accumulated in place. The &#96;count&#96; operator promotes the accumulated\n    /// values to data, at which point a final map applies operator-specific logic to\n    /// yield the final aggregate.\n    fn build_accumulable&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        collection: VecCollection&lt;S, (Row, Row), Diff&gt;,",

        "contents": "                    }\n                },\n            );\n        (\n            arranged_output,\n            arranged_errs.as_collection(|_key, error| error.clone()),\n        )\n    }\n}\n\n/// Evaluates the fused MFP, if one exists, on a reconstructed &#96;DatumVecBorrow&#96;\n/// containing key and aggregate values, then returns a result &#96;Row&#96; or &#96;None&#96;\n/// if the MFP filters the result out.\nfn evaluate_mfp_after&lt;'a, 'b&gt;(\n    mfp_after: &amp;'a Option&lt;SafeMfpPlan&gt;,\n    datums_local: &amp;'b mut mz_repr::DatumVecBorrow&lt;'a&gt;,\n    temp_storage: &amp;'a RowArena,\n    key_len: usize,\n) -&gt; Option&lt;Row&gt; {\n    let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n    // Apply MFP if it exists and pack a Row of\n    // aggregate values from &#96;datums_local&#96;.\n    if let Some(mfp) = mfp_after {\n        // It must ignore errors here, but they are scanned\n        // for elsewhere if the MFP can error.\n        if let Ok(Some(iter)) = mfp.evaluate_iter(datums_local, temp_storage) {\n            // The &#96;mfp_after&#96; must preserve the key columns,\n            // so we can skip them to form aggregation results.\n            Some(row_builder.pack_using(iter.skip(key_len)))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    } else {\n        Some(row_builder.pack_using(&amp;datums_local[key_len..]))\n    }\n}\n\nfn accumulable_zero(aggr_func: &amp;AggregateFunc) -&gt; Accum",
