```json
        "contents": "                    if (accumulable.next(), hierarchical.next(), basic.next()) != (None, None, None)\n                    {\n                        let message = \"Rows too large for key in ReduceCollation\";\n                        error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"key={key:?}\"));\n                        output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n\n                    // Finally, if &#96;mfp_after&#96; can produce errors, then we should also report\n                    // these here.\n                    let Some(mfp) = &amp;mfp_after2 else { return };\n                    if let Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage) {\n                        output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n                },\n            );\n        (oks, errs.as_collection(|_, v| v.clone()))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the dataflow to compute the set of distinct keys.\n    fn build_distinct&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        collection:",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/logging/compute.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1439,
            "column": 13
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1495,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "as_collection()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;G, B&gt; LogDataflowErrors for Stream&lt;G, B&gt;\nwhere\n    G: Scope,\n    for&lt;'a&gt; B: BatchReader&lt;DiffGat&lt;'a&gt; = &amp;'a Diff&gt; + Clone + 'static,\n{\n    fn log_dataflow_errors(self, logger: Logger, export_id: GlobalId) -&gt; Self {\n        self.unary(Pipeline, \"LogDataflowErrorsStream\", |_cap, _info| {\n            move |input, output| {\n                input.for_each(|cap, data| {\n                    let diff = data.iter().map(sum_batch_diffs).sum::&lt;Diff&gt;();\n                    logger.log(&amp;ComputeEvent::ErrorCount(ErrorCount { export_id, diff }));\n\n                    output.session(&amp;cap).give_container(data);\n                });\n            }\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Return the sum of all diffs within the given batch.\n///\n/// Note that this operation can be expensive: Its runtime is O(N) with N being the number of\n/// unique (key, value, time) tuples. We only use it on error streams, which are expected to\n/// contain only a small number of records, so this doesn't matter much. But avoid using it when\n/// batches might become large.\nfn sum_batch_diffs&lt;B&gt;(batch: &amp;B) -&gt; Diff\nwhere\n    for&lt;'a&gt; B: BatchReader&lt;DiffGat&lt;'a&gt; = &amp;'a Diff&gt;,\n{\n    let mut sum = Diff::ZERO;\n    let mut cursor = batch.cursor();\n\n    while cursor.key_valid(batch) {\n        while cursor.val_valid(batch) {\n            cursor.map_times(batch, |_t, r| sum += r);\n            cursor.step_val(batch);\n        }\n        cursor.step_key(batch);\n    }\n\n    sum\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[mz_ore::test]\n    fn test_compute_event_size() {\n        // This could be a static assertion, but we don't use those yet in this crate.\n        assert_eq!(56, std::mem::size_of::&lt;ComputeEvent&gt;())\n    }\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1441

          },

          },

            "text": "where",

            "text": "    G: Scope,",

          {

            "text": "/// Return the sum of all diffs within the given batch.",

        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Helpers for handling errors encountered by operators.\n\nuse mz_repr::Row;\n\n/// Used to make possibly-validating code generic: think of this as a kind of &#96;MaybeResult&#96;,\n/// specialized for use in compute.  Validation code will only run when the error constructor is\n/// Some.\npub(super) trait MaybeValidatingRow&lt;T, E&gt; {\n    fn ok(t: T) -&gt; Self;\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt;;\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;Row, E&gt; for Row {\n    fn ok(t: Row) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;(), E&gt; for () {\n    fn ok(t: ()) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E, R&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;Vec&lt;R&gt;, E&gt; for Vec&lt;R&gt; {\n    fn ok(t: Vec&lt;R&gt;) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;T, E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;T, E&gt; for Result&lt;T, E&gt; {\n    fn ok(row: T) -&gt; Self {\n        Ok(row)\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        Some(Err)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error logger to be used by rendering code.\n// TODO: Consider removing this struct.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub(super) struct ErrorLogger {\n    dataflow_name: String,\n}\n\nimpl ErrorLogger {\n    pub fn new(dataflow_name: String) -&gt; Self",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "    {\n        let error_logger = self.error_logger();\n        // NOTE(vmarcos): The input operator name below is used in the tuning advice built-in\n        // view mz_introspection.mz_expected_group_size_advice.\n        let arranged_input = input\n            .mz_arrange::&lt;RowRowBatcher&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                \"Arranged MinsMaxesHierarchical input\",\n            );\n\n        let reduced = arranged_input.mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, Bu, Tr&gt;(\n            \"Reduced Fallibly MinsMaxesHierarchical\",\n            move |key, source, target| {\n                if let Some(err) = Tr::ValOwn::into_error() {\n                    // Should negative accumulations reach us, we should loudly complain.\n                    for (value, count) in source.iter() {\n                        if count.is_positive() {\n                            continue;\n                        }\n                        error_logger.log(\n                            \"Non-positive accumulation in MinsMaxesHierarchical\",\n                            &amp;format!(\"key={key:?}, value={value:?}, count={count}\"),\n                        );\n                        // After complaining, output an error here so that we can eventually\n                        // report it in an error stream.\n                        target.push((err(Tr::owned_key(key)), Diff::ONE));\n                        return;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n                let mut row_packer = row_builder.packer();\n\n                let mut source_iters = source\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|(values, _cnt)| *values)\n                    .collect::&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;&gt;();\n                for func in aggrs.iter() {\n                    let column_iter =\n                        (0..source_iters.len()).map(|i| source_iters[i].next().unwrap());\n                    row_packer.push(func.eval(column_iter, &amp;RowArena::new()));\n                }\n                // We only want to arrange the parts of the input that are not part of the output.\n                // More specifically, we want to arrange it so that &#96;input.concat(&amp;output.negate())&#96;",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "                    for (item, _) in input.iter() {\n                        let reduction_type = &amp;item.0;\n                        let row = &amp;item.1;\n                        match reduction_type {\n                            ReductionType::Accumulable =&gt; accumulable = row.iter(),\n                            ReductionType::Hierarchical =&gt; hierarchical = row.iter(),\n                            ReductionType::Basic =&gt; basic = row.iter(),\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                    let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                    let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                    datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n\n                    for typ in aggregate_types_err.iter() {\n                        let datum = match typ {\n                            ReductionType::Accumulable =&gt; accumulable.next(),\n                            ReductionType::Hierarchical =&gt; hierarchical.next(),\n                            ReductionType::Basic =&gt; basic.next(),\n                        };\n                        if let Some(datum) = datum {\n                            datums_local.push(datum);\n                        } else {\n                            // We cannot properly reconstruct a row if aggregates are missing.\n                            // This situation is not expected, so we log an error if it occurs.\n                            let message = \"Missing value for key in ReduceCollation\";\n                            error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"typ={typ:?}, key={key:?}\"));\n                            output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                            return;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Note that we also do not want to have anything left over to stich.\n                    // If we do, then we also have an error and would violate join semantics.\n                    if (accumulable.next(), hierarchical.next(), basic.next()) != (None, None, None)\n                    {",

      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 1628,
            "column": 89
          }
        },
        "contents": "                move |key, input, output| {\n                    let (ref accums, total) = input[0].1;\n                    for (aggr, accum) in err_full_aggrs.iter().zip_eq(accums) {\n                        // We first test here if inputs without net-positive records are present,\n                        // producing an error to the logs and to the query output if that is the case.\n                        if total == Diff::ZERO &amp;&amp; !accum.is_zero() {\n                            error_logger.log(\n                                \"Net-zero records with non-zero accumulation in ReduceAccumulable\",\n                                &amp;format!(\"aggr={aggr:?}, accum={accum:?}\"),\n                            );\n                            let key = key.to_row();\n                            let message = format!(\n                                \"Invalid data in source, saw net-zero records for key {key} \\\n                                 with non-zero accumulation in accumulable aggregate\"\n                            );\n                            output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                        }\n                        match (&amp;aggr.func, &amp;accum) {\n                            (AggregateFunc::SumUInt16, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. })",
```

```json
        "contents": "    fn render_plan_expr(\n        &amp;mut self,\n        expr: render_plan::Expr,\n        collections: &amp;BTreeMap&lt;LirId, CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt;&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; CollectionBundle&lt;G&gt; {\n        use render_plan::Expr::*;\n\n        let expect_input = |id| {\n            collections\n                .get(&amp;id)\n                .cloned()\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| panic!(\"missing input collection: {id}\"))\n        };\n\n        match expr {\n            Constant { rows } =&gt; {\n                // Produce both rows and errs to avoid conditional dataflow construction.\n                let (rows, errs) = match rows {\n                    Ok(rows) =&gt; (rows, Vec::new()),\n                    Err(e) =&gt; (Vec::new(), vec![e]),\n                };\n\n                // We should advance times in constant collections to start from &#96;as_of&#96;.\n                let as_of_frontier = self.as_of_frontier.clone();\n                let until = self.until.clone();\n                let ok_collection = rows\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .filter_map(move |(row, mut time, diff)| {\n                        time.advance_by(as_of_frontier.borrow());\n                        if !until.less_equal(&amp;time) {\n                            Some((\n                                row,\n                                &lt;G::Timestamp as Refines&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;&gt;::to_inner(time),\n                                diff,\n                            ))\n                        } else {\n                            None\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .to_stream(&amp;mut self.scope)\n                    .as_collection();\n\n                let mut error_time: mz_repr::Timestamp = Timestamp::minimum();\n                error_time.advance_by(self.as_of_frontier.borrow());\n                let err_collection = errs\n                    .into_iter()",

        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Renders a plan into a timely/differential dataflow computation.\n//!\n//! ## Error handling\n//!\n//! Timely and differential have no idioms for computations that can error. The\n//! philosophy is, reasonably, to define the semantics of the computation such\n//! that errors are unnecessary: e.g., by using wrap-around semantics for\n//! integer overflow.\n//!\n//! Unfortunately, SQL semantics are not nearly so elegant, and require errors\n//! in myriad cases. The classic example is a division by zero, but invalid\n//! input for casts, overflowing integer operations, and dozens of other\n//! functions need the ability to produce errors ar runtime.\n//!\n//! At the moment, only *scalar* expression evaluation can fail, so only\n//! operators that evaluate scalar expressions can fail. At the time of writing,\n//! that includes map, filter, reduce, and join operators. Constants are a bit\n//! of a special case: they can be either a constant vector of rows *or* a\n//! constant, singular error.\n//!\n//! The approach taken is to build two parallel trees of computation: one for\n//! the rows that have been successfully evaluated (the \"oks tree\"), and one for\n//! the errors that have been generated (the \"errs tree\"). For example:\n//!\n//! &#96;&#96;&#96;text\n//!    oks1  errs1       oks2  errs2\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!   project  |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!      |     |           |     |\n//!     map    |           |     |\n//!      |\\    |           |     |\n//!      | \\   |           |     |\n//!      |  \\  |           |     |\n//!      |   \\ |           |     |\n//!      |    \\|           |     |\n//!   project  +           +     +",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "//",

          },

            "text": "// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License",

            "text": "// included in the LICENSE file.",

            "text": "//",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },

            "lineNumber": 6

            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Renders a plan into a timely/differential dataflow computation.",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "//! ## Error handling",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Timely and differential have no idioms for computations that can error. The",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "//! philosophy is, reasonably, to define the semantics of the computation such",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "//! that errors are unnecessary: e.g., by using wrap-around semantics for",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "//! integer overflow.",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Unfortunately, SQL semantics are not nearly so elegant, and require errors",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "//! in myriad cases. The classic example is a division by zero, but invalid",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "//! input for casts, overflowing integer operations, and dozens of other",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "//! functions need the ability to produce errors ar runtime.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "//! At the moment, only *scalar* expression evaluation can fail, so only",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "//! operators that evaluate scalar expressions can fail. At the time of writing,",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "//! that includes map, filter, reduce, and join operators. Constants are a bit",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "//! of a special case: they can be either a constant vector of rows *or* a",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "//! constant, singular error.",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "//! The approach taken is to build two parallel trees of computation: one for",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "//! the rows that have been successfully evaluated (the \"oks tree\"), and one for",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "//! the errors that have been generated (the \"errs tree\"). For example:",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "//! &#96;&#96;&#96;text",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "//!    oks1  errs1       oks2  errs2",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |     |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |     |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "//!   project  |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |     |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |     |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "//!     map    |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |\\    |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      | \\   |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |  \\  |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |   \\ |           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "//!      |    \\|           |     |",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "//!   project  +           +     +",
            "lineNumber": 47
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3119758367538452
    },

        "contents": "            // (database-issues#7108).\n\n            match top_k_plan.limit().map(|l| (l.as_literal(), l)) {\n                None =&gt; {}\n                Some((Some(Ok(literal)), _))\n                    if literal == Datum::Null || literal.unwrap_int64() &gt;= 0 =&gt; {}\n                Some((_, expr)) =&gt; {\n                    // Produce errors from limit selectors that error or are\n                    // negative, and nothing from limit selectors that do\n                    // not. Note that even if expr.could_error() is false,\n                    // the expression might still return a negative limit and\n                    // thus needs to be checked.\n                    let expr = expr.clone();\n                    let mut datum_vec = mz_repr::DatumVec::new();\n                    let errors = ok_input.flat_map(move |row| {\n                        let temp_storage = mz_repr::RowArena::new();\n                        let datums = datum_vec.borrow_with(&amp;row);\n                        match expr.eval(&amp;datums[..], &amp;temp_storage) {\n                            Ok(l) if l != Datum::Null &amp;&amp; l.unwrap_int64() &lt; 0 =&gt; {\n                                Some(EvalError::NegLimit.into())\n                            }\n                            Ok(_) =&gt; None,\n                            Err(e) =&gt; Some(e.into()),\n                        }\n                    });\n                    err_collection = err_collection.concat(&amp;errors);\n                }\n            }\n\n            let ok_result =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/sinks.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 151,
            "column": 20
          }
        },
        "contents": "        if !non_null_assertions.is_empty() {\n            let name = format!(\"NullAssertions({sink_id:?})\");\n            type CB&lt;C&gt; = CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;C&gt;;\n            let (oks, null_errs) =\n                ok_collection.map_fallible::&lt;CB&lt;_&gt;, CB&lt;_&gt;, _, _, _&gt;(&amp;name, move |row| {\n                    let mut idx = 0;\n                    let mut iter = row.iter();\n                    for &amp;i in &amp;non_null_assertions {\n                        let skip = i - idx;\n                        let datum = iter.nth(skip).unwrap();\n                        idx += skip + 1;\n                        if datum.is_null() {\n                            return Err(DataflowError::EvalError(Box::new(\n                                EvalError::MustNotBeNull(\n                                    format!(\"column {}\", from_desc.get_name(i).quoted()).into(),\n                                ),\n                            )));\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Ok(row)\n                });\n            ok_collection = oks;\n            err_collection = err_collection.concat(&amp;null_errs);\n        }\n\n        let region_name = match sink.connection {\n            ComputeSinkConnection::Subscribe(_) =&gt; format!(\"SubscribeSink({:?})\", sink_id),\n            ComputeSinkConnection::MaterializedView(_) =&gt; {\n                format!(\"MaterializedViewSink({:?})\", sink_id)\n            }\n            ComputeSinkConnection::ContinualTask(_) =&gt; {\n                format!(\"ContinualTaskSink({:?})\", sink_id)\n            }\n            ComputeSinkConnection::CopyToS3Oneshot(_) =&gt; {\n                format!(\"CopyToS3OneshotSink({:?})\", sink_id)\n            }\n        };\n        self.scope\n            .parent\n            .clone()",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/context.rs",
        "range": {

          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 780,
            "column": 17
          }
        },
        "contents": "        let (stream, errors) = self.flat_map(key_val, max_demand, move |row_datums, time, diff| {\n            let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n            let until = std::rc::Rc::clone(&amp;until);\n            let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n            let row_iter = row_datums.iter();\n            let mut datums_local = datum_vec.borrow();\n            datums_local.extend(row_iter);\n            let time = time.clone();\n            let event_time = time.event_time();\n            mfp_plan\n                .evaluate(\n                    &amp;mut datums_local,\n                    &amp;temp_storage,\n                    event_time,\n                    diff.clone(),\n                    move |time| !until.less_equal(time),\n                    &amp;mut row_builder,\n                )\n                .map(move |x| match x {\n                    Ok((row, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                        // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                        let mut time: S::Timestamp = time.clone();\n                        *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                        (Ok(row), time, diff)\n                    }\n                    Err((e, event_time, diff)) =&gt; {\n                        // Copy the whole time, and re-populate event time.\n                        let mut time: S::Timestamp = time.clone();\n                        *time.event_time_mut() = event_time;\n                        (Err(e), time, diff)\n                    }\n                })\n        });\n\n        use differential_dataflow::AsCollection;\n        let (oks, errs) = stream\n            .as_collection()\n            .map_fallible::&lt;CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;_&gt;, CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;_&gt;, _, _, _&gt;(\n                \"OkErr\",\n                |x| x,\n            );\n\n        (oks, errors.concat(&amp;errs))\n    }\n    pub fn ensure_collections(\n        mut self,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/top_k.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 405,
            "column": 75
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 449,
            "column": 14
          }
        },
        "contents": "        let (input, oks, errs) = if validating {\n            // Build topk stage, produce errors for invalid multiplicities.\n            let (input, stage) = build_topk_negated_stage::&lt;\n                S,\n                RowValBuilder&lt;_, _, _&gt;,\n                RowValSpine&lt;Result&lt;Row, Row&gt;, _, _&gt;,\n            &gt;(&amp;input, order_key, offset, limit, arity);\n            let stage = stage.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.clone()));\n\n            // Demux oks and errors.\n            let error_logger = self.error_logger();\n            type CB&lt;C&gt; = CapacityContainerBuilder&lt;C&gt;;\n            let (oks, errs) = stage.map_fallible::&lt;CB&lt;_&gt;, CB&lt;_&gt;, _, _, _&gt;(\n                \"Demuxing Errors\",\n                move |(hk, result)| match result {\n                    Err(v) =&gt; {\n                        let mut hk_iter = hk.iter();\n                        let h = hk_iter.next().unwrap().unwrap_uint64();\n                        let k = SharedRow::pack(hk_iter);\n                        let message = \"Negative multiplicities in TopK\";\n                        error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"k={k:?}, h={h}, v={v:?}\"));\n                        Err(EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into())\n                    }\n                    Ok(t) =&gt; Ok((hk, t)),\n                },\n            );\n            (input, oks, Some(errs))\n        } else {\n            // Build non-validating topk stage.\n            let (input, stage) =\n                build_topk_negated_stage::&lt;S, RowRowBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                    &amp;input, order_key, offset, limit, arity,\n                );\n            // Turn arrangement into collection.\n            let stage = stage.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.to_row()));\n\n            (input, stage, None)\n        };\n        let input = input.as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.to_row()));\n        (oks.concat(&amp;input), errs)\n    }\n\n    fn render_top1_monotonic&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "        let (input, oks, errs) = if validating {",
            "lineNumber": 407
          },
          {
            "text": "            // Build topk stage, produce errors for invalid multiplicities.",
            "lineNumber": 408
          },
          {
            "text": "            let (input, stage) = build_topk_negated_stage::&lt;",
            "lineNumber": 409
          },
          {
            "text": "                S,",
            "lineNumber": 410

        "contents": "(\"Reduced TopK input\", {\n        move |mut hash_key, source, target: &amp;mut Vec&lt;(Tr::ValOwn, Diff)&gt;| {\n            // Unpack the limit, either into an integer literal or an expression to evaluate.\n            let limit = match &amp;limit {\n                Some(Ok(lit)) =&gt; Some(*lit),\n                Some(Err(expr)) =&gt; {\n                    // Unpack &#96;key&#96; after skipping the hash and determine the limit.\n                    // If the limit errors, use a zero limit; errors are surfaced elsewhere.\n                    let temp_storage = mz_repr::RowArena::new();\n                    let _hash = hash_key.next();\n                    let mut key_datums = datum_vec.borrow();\n                    key_datums.extend(hash_key);\n                    let datum_limit = expr\n                        .eval(&amp;key_datums, &amp;temp_storage)\n                        .unwrap_or(Datum::Int64(0));\n                    Some(match datum_limit {\n                        Datum::Null =&gt; Diff::MAX,\n                        d =&gt; Diff::from(d.unwrap_int64()),\n                    })\n                }\n                None =&gt; None,\n            };\n\n            if let Some(err) = Tr::ValOwn::into_error() {\n                for (datums, diff) in source.iter() {\n                    if diff.is_positive() {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    target.push((err((*datums).to_row()), Diff::ONE));\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Determine if we must actually shrink the result set.\n            let must_shrink = offset &gt; 0\n                || limit\n                    .map(|l| source.iter().map(|(_, d)| *d).sum::&lt;Diff&gt;() &gt; l)\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n            if !must_shrink {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            // First go ahead and emit all records. Note that we ensure target\n            // has the capacity to hold at least these records, and avoid any\n            // dependencies on the user-provided (potentially unbounded) limit.\n            target.reserve(source.len());\n            for (datums, diff) in source.iter()",

        "contents": "                    for (item, _) in input.iter() {\n                        let reduction_type = &amp;item.0;\n                        let row = &amp;item.1;\n                        match reduction_type {\n                            ReductionType::Accumulable =&gt; accumulable = row.iter(),\n                            ReductionType::Hierarchical =&gt; hierarchical = row.iter(),\n                            ReductionType::Basic =&gt; basic = row.iter(),\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                    let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                    let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                    datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n\n                    for typ in aggregate_types_err.iter() {\n                        let datum = match typ {\n                            ReductionType::Accumulable =&gt; accumulable.next(),\n                            ReductionType::Hierarchical =&gt; hierarchical.next(),\n                            ReductionType::Basic =&gt; basic.next(),\n                        };\n                        if let Some(datum) = datum {\n                            datums_local.push(datum);\n                        } else {\n                            // We cannot properly reconstruct a row if aggregates are missing.\n                            // This situation is not expected, so we log an error if it occurs.\n                            let message = \"Missing value for key in ReduceCollation\";\n                            error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"typ={typ:?}, key={key:?}\"));\n                            output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                            return;\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // Note that we also do not want to have anything left over to stich.\n                    // If we do, then we also have an error and would violate join semantics.\n                    if (accumulable.next(), hierarchical.next(), basic.next()) != (None, None, None)\n                    {",

        "contents": "        'collect: while limit_remaining &gt; 0 {\n            let Some(batch) = cursor.next().await else {\n                break;\n            };\n            for (data, _, d) in batch {\n                let row = data.map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n\n                if let Some(literal) = &amp;literal_constraint {\n                    match row.iter().take(literal_len).cmp(literal.iter()) {\n                        Ordering::Less =&gt; continue,\n                        Ordering::Equal =&gt; {}\n                        Ordering::Greater =&gt; break 'collect,\n                    }\n                }\n\n                let count: usize = d.try_into().map_err(|_| {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        shard = %metadata.data_shard, diff = d, ?row,\n                        \"persist peek encountered negative multiplicities\",\n                    );\n                    format!(\n                        \"Invalid data in source, \\\n                         saw retractions ({}) for row that does not exist: {:?}\",\n                        -d, row,\n                    )\n                })?;\n                let Some(count) = NonZeroUsize::new(count) else {\n                    continue;\n                };\n                let mut datum_local = datum_vec.borrow_with(&amp;row);\n                let eval_result = mfp_plan\n                    .evaluate_into(&amp;mut datum_local, &amp;arena, &amp;mut row_builder)\n                    .map(|row| row.cloned())\n                    .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n                if let Some(row) = eval_result {\n                    total_size = total_size\n                        .",

        "contents": "    ) {\n        // put together tokens that belong to the export\n        let mut needed_tokens = Vec::new();\n        for dep_id in dependency_ids {\n            if let Some(token) = tokens.get(&amp;dep_id) {\n                needed_tokens.push(Rc::clone(token));\n            }\n        }\n        let bundle = self.lookup_id(Id::Global(idx_id)).unwrap_or_else(|| {\n            panic!(\n                \"Arrangement alarmingly absent! id: {:?}\",\n                Id::Global(idx_id)\n            )\n        });\n\n        match bundle.arrangement(&amp;idx.key) {\n            Some(ArrangementFlavor::Local(oks, errs)) =&gt; {\n                // TODO: The following as_collection/leave/arrange sequence could be optimized.\n                //   * Combine as_collection and leave into a single function.\n                //   * Use columnar to extract columns from the batches to implement leave.\n                let mut oks = oks\n                    .as_collection(|k, v| (k.to_row(), v.to_row()))\n                    .leave()\n                    .mz_arrange::&lt;RowRowBatcher&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, _&gt;(\n                    \"Arrange export iterative\",\n                );\n\n                let mut errs = errs\n                    .as_collection(|k, v| (k.clone(), v.clone()))\n                    .leave()\n                    .mz_arrange::&lt;ErrBatcher&lt;_, _&gt;, ErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, _&gt;(\n                        \"Arrange export iterative err\",\n                    );\n\n                // Ensure that the frontier does not advance past the expiration time, if set.\n                // Otherwise, we might write down incorrect data.\n                if let Some(&amp;expiration) = self.dataflow_expiration.as_option() {\n                    oks.stream = oks.stream.expire_stream_at",

        "contents": "                    if (accumulable.next(), hierarchical.next(), basic.next()) != (None, None, None)\n                    {\n                        let message = \"Rows too large for key in ReduceCollation\";\n                        error_logger.log(message, &amp;format!(\"key={key:?}\"));\n                        output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n\n                    // Finally, if &#96;mfp_after&#96; can produce errors, then we should also report\n                    // these here.\n                    let Some(mfp) = &amp;mfp_after2 else { return };\n                    if let Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage) {\n                        output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n                },\n            );\n        (oks, errs.as_collection(|_, v| v.clone()))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the dataflow to compute the set of distinct keys.\n    fn build_distinct&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        collection:",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "            if must_validate || mfp_after2.is_some() {\n                let errs = arranged\n                    .mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, RowErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowErrSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                        \"ReduceMinsMaxes Error Check\",\n                        move |key, source, target| {\n                            // Negative counts would be surprising, but until we are 100% certain we wont\n                            // see them, we should report when we do. We may want to bake even more info\n                            // in here in the future.\n                            if must_validate {\n                                for (val, count) in source.iter() {\n                                    if count.is_positive() {\n                                        continue;\n                                    }\n                                    let val = val.to_row();\n                                    let message = \"Non-positive accumulation in ReduceMinsMaxes\";\n                                    error_logger\n                                        .log(message, &amp;format!(\"val={val:?}, count={count}\"));\n                                    target.push((\n                                        EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(),\n                                        Diff::ONE,\n                                    ));\n                                    return;\n                                }\n                            }\n\n                            // We know that &#96;mfp_after&#96; can error if it exists, so try to evaluate it here.\n                            let Some(mfp) = &amp;mfp_after2 else { return };\n                            let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                            let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                            let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                            datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n\n                            let mut source_iters =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/continual_task.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 419,

            "line": 474,
            "column": 13
          }
        },
        "contents": "    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;G&gt; SinkRender&lt;G&gt; for ContinualTaskConnection&lt;CollectionMetadata&gt;\nwhere\n    G: Scope&lt;Timestamp = Timestamp&gt;,\n{\n    fn render_sink(\n        &amp;self,\n        compute_state: &amp;mut ComputeState,\n        _sink: &amp;ComputeSinkDesc&lt;CollectionMetadata&gt;,\n        sink_id: GlobalId,\n        as_of: Antichain&lt;Timestamp&gt;,\n        start_signal: StartSignal,\n        oks: VecCollection&lt;G, Row, Diff&gt;,\n        errs: VecCollection&lt;G, DataflowError, Diff&gt;,\n        append_times: Option&lt;VecCollection&lt;G, (), Diff&gt;&gt;,\n        flow_control_probe: &amp;probe::Handle&lt;Timestamp&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; Option&lt;Rc&lt;dyn Any&gt;&gt; {\n        let name = sink_id.to_string();\n\n        let to_append = oks\n            .map(|x| SourceData(Ok(x)))\n            .concat(&amp;errs.map(|x| SourceData(Err(x))));\n        let append_times = append_times.expect(\"should be provided by ContinualTaskCtx\");\n\n        let write_handle = {\n            let clients = Arc::clone(&amp;compute_state.persist_clients);\n            let metadata = self.storage_metadata.clone();\n            let handle_purpose = format!(\"ct_sink({})\", name);\n            async move {\n                let client = clients\n                    .open(metadata.persist_location)\n                    .await\n                    .expect(\"valid location\");\n                client\n                    .open_writer(\n                        metadata.data_shard,\n                        metadata.relation_desc.into(),\n                        UnitSchema.into(),\n                        Diagnostics {\n                            shard_name: sink_id.to_string(),\n                            handle_purpose,\n                        },\n                    )\n                    .await\n                    .expect(\"codecs should match\")\n            }\n        };\n\n        let collection = compute_state.expect_collection_mut(sink_id);\n        let probe = ProbeHandle::default();\n        let to_append = to_append\n            .probe_with(&amp;probe)\n            .",

        "contents": "            for (source_id, import) in dataflow.source_imports.iter() {\n                region.region_named(&amp;format!(\"Source({:?})\", source_id), |inner| {\n                    let mut read_schema = None;\n                    let mut mfp = import.desc.arguments.operators.clone().map(|mut ops| {\n                        // If enabled, we read from Persist with a &#96;RelationDesc&#96; that\n                        // omits uneeded columns.\n                        if apply_demands {\n                            let demands = ops.demand();\n                            let new_desc = import\n                                .desc\n                                .storage_metadata\n                                .relation_desc\n                                .apply_demand(&amp;demands);\n                            let new_arity = demands.len();\n                            let remap: BTreeMap&lt;_, _&gt; = demands\n                                .into_iter()\n                                .enumerate()\n                                .map(|(new, old)| (old, new))\n                                .collect();\n                            ops.permute_fn(|old_idx| remap[&amp;old_idx], new_arity);\n                            read_schema = Some(new_desc);\n                        }\n\n                        mz_expr::MfpPlan::create_from(ops)\n                            .expect(\"Linear operators should always be valid\")\n                    });\n\n                    let mut snapshot_mode =\n                        if import.with_snapshot || !subscribe_snapshot_optimization {\n                            SnapshotMode::Include\n                        } else {\n                            compute_state.metrics.inc_subscribe_snapshot_optimization();\n                            SnapshotMode::Exclude\n                        };\n                    let mut suppress_early_progress_as_of = dataflow.as_of.clone();\n                    let ct_source_transformer = ct_ctx.get_ct_source_transformer(*source_id);\n                    if let Some(x) = ct_source_transformer.as_ref() {\n                        snapshot_mode = x.snapshot_mode();",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/continual_task.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 267,
            "column": 20
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 302,
            "column": 49
          }
        },
        "contents": "&lt;S: Scope&lt;Timestamp = Timestamp&gt;&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        oks: VecCollection&lt;S, Row, Diff&gt;,\n        errs: VecCollection&lt;S, DataflowError, Diff&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; (\n        VecCollection&lt;S, Row, Diff&gt;,\n        VecCollection&lt;S, DataflowError, Diff&gt;,\n        VecCollection&lt;S, (), Diff&gt;,\n    ) {\n        use ContinualTaskSourceTransformer::*;\n        match self {\n            // Make a collection s.t, for each time T in the input, the output\n            // contains the inserts at T.\n            InsertsInput { source_id, .. } =&gt; {\n                let name = source_id.to_string();\n                // Keep only the inserts.\n                let oks = oks.inner.filter(|(_, _, diff)| diff.is_positive());\n                // Grab the original times for use in the sink operator.\n                let (oks, times) = oks.as_collection().times_extract(&amp;name);\n                // Then retract everything at the next timestamp.\n                let oks = oks.inner.flat_map(|(row, ts, diff)| {\n                    let retract_ts = ts.step_forward();\n                    let negation = -diff;\n                    [(row.clone(), ts, diff), (row, retract_ts, negation)]\n                });\n                (oks.as_collection(), errs, times)\n            }\n            NormalReference =&gt; {\n                let times = VecCollection::empty(&amp;oks.scope());\n                (oks, errs, times)\n            }\n            // When computing an self-referential output at &#96;T&#96;, start by\n            // assuming there are no changes from the contents at &#96;T-1&#96;. See the\n            // module rustdoc for how this fits into the larger picture.\n            SelfReference { source_id } =&gt; {\n                let name = source_id.to_string();",

        "contents": "    {\n        let error_logger = self.error_logger();\n        // NOTE(vmarcos): The input operator name below is used in the tuning advice built-in\n        // view mz_introspection.mz_expected_group_size_advice.\n        let arranged_input = input\n            .mz_arrange::&lt;RowRowBatcher&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowRowSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                \"Arranged MinsMaxesHierarchical input\",\n            );\n\n        let reduced = arranged_input.mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, Bu, Tr&gt;(\n            \"Reduced Fallibly MinsMaxesHierarchical\",\n            move |key, source, target| {\n                if let Some(err) = Tr::ValOwn::into_error() {\n                    // Should negative accumulations reach us, we should loudly complain.\n                    for (value, count) in source.iter() {\n                        if count.is_positive() {\n                            continue;\n                        }\n                        error_logger.log(\n                            \"Non-positive accumulation in MinsMaxesHierarchical\",\n                            &amp;format!(\"key={key:?}, value={value:?}, count={count}\"),\n                        );\n                        // After complaining, output an error here so that we can eventually\n                        // report it in an error stream.\n                        target.push((err(Tr::owned_key(key)), Diff::ONE));\n                        return;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n                let mut row_packer = row_builder.packer();\n\n                let mut source_iters = source\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|(values, _cnt)| *values)\n                    .collect::&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;&gt;();\n                for func in aggrs.iter() {\n                    let column_iter =\n                        (0..source_iters.len()).map(|i| source_iters[i].next().unwrap());\n                    row_packer.push(func.eval(column_iter, &amp;RowArena::new()));\n                }\n                // We only want to arrange the parts of the input that are not part of the output.\n                // More specifically, we want to arrange it so that &#96;input.concat(&amp;output.negate())&#96;",

        "contents": "            for path_plan in join_plan.path_plans.iter() {\n                for stage_plan in path_plan.stage_plans.iter() {\n                    let lookup_idx = stage_plan.lookup_relation;\n                    let lookup_key = stage_plan.lookup_key.clone();\n                    arrangements\n                        .entry((lookup_idx, lookup_key.clone()))\n                        .or_insert_with(|| {\n                            match inputs[lookup_idx]\n                                .arrangement(&amp;lookup_key)\n                                .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                                    panic!(\n                                        \"Arrangement alarmingly absent!: {}, {:?}\",\n                                        lookup_idx, lookup_key,\n                                    )\n                                }) {\n                                ArrangementFlavor::Local(oks, errs) =&gt; {\n                                    if err_dedup.insert((lookup_idx, lookup_key)) {\n                                        inner_errs.push(\n                                            errs.enter_region(inner)\n                                                .as_collection(|k, _v| k.clone()),\n                                        );\n                                    }\n                                    Ok(oks.enter_region(inner))\n                                }\n                                ArrangementFlavor::Trace(_gid, oks, errs) =&gt;         "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Helpers for handling errors encountered by operators.\n\nuse mz_repr::Row;\n\n/// Used to make possibly-validating code generic: think of this as a kind of &#96;MaybeResult&#96;,\n/// specialized for use in compute.  Validation code will only run when the error constructor is\n/// Some.\npub(super) trait MaybeValidatingRow&lt;T, E&gt; {\n    fn ok(t: T) -&gt; Self;\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt;;\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;Row, E&gt; for Row {\n    fn ok(t: Row) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;(), E&gt; for () {\n    fn ok(t: ()) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;E, R&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;Vec&lt;R&gt;, E&gt; for Vec&lt;R&gt; {\n    fn ok(t: Vec&lt;R&gt;) -&gt; Self {\n        t\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;T, E&gt; MaybeValidatingRow&lt;T, E&gt; for Result&lt;T, E&gt; {\n    fn ok(row: T) -&gt; Self {\n        Ok(row)\n    }\n\n    fn into_error() -&gt; Option&lt;fn(E) -&gt; Self&gt; {\n        Some(Err)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Error logger to be used by rendering code.\n// TODO: Consider removing this struct.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub(super) struct ErrorLogger {\n    dataflow_name: String,\n}\n\nimpl ErrorLogger {\n    pub fn new(dataflow_name: String) -&gt; Self",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 1006,
            "column": 34
          }
        },
        "contents": "                let errs = err\n                    .mz_arrange::&lt;ErrBatcher&lt;_, _&gt;, ErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, ErrSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                        \"Arrange recursive err\",\n                    )\n                    .mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, ErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, ErrSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                        \"Distinct recursive err\",\n                        move |_k, _s, t| t.push(((), Diff::ONE)),\n                    )\n                    .as_collection(|k, _| k.clone());\n\n                oks_v.set(&amp;oks);\n                err_v.set(&amp;errs);\n            }\n            // Now extract each of the rec bindings into the outer scope.\n            for id in rec_ids.into_iter() {\n                let bundle = self.remove_id(Id::Local(id)).unwrap();\n                let (oks, err) = bundle.collection.unwrap();\n                self.insert_id(\n                    Id::Local(id),\n                    CollectionBundle::from_collections(\n                        oks.leave_dynamic(level + 1),\n                        err.leave_dynamic(level + 1),\n                    ),\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.render_letfree_plan(object_id, plan.body, binding)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;G&gt; Context&lt;G&gt;\nwhere\n    G: Scope,\n    G::Timestamp: RenderTimestamp,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/join/linear_join.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 466,
            "column": 43
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 518,
            "column": 37
          }
        },
        "contents": "(\n        &amp;self,\n        prev_keyed: Arranged&lt;S, Tr1&gt;,\n        next_input: Arranged&lt;S, Tr2&gt;,\n        closure: JoinClosure,\n    ) -&gt; (\n        VecCollection&lt;S, Row, Diff&gt;,\n        Option&lt;VecCollection&lt;S, DataflowError, Diff&gt;&gt;,\n    )\n    where\n        S: Scope&lt;Timestamp = G::Timestamp&gt;,\n        Tr1: TraceReader&lt;Time = G::Timestamp, Diff = Diff&gt; + Clone + 'static,\n        Tr2: for&lt;'a&gt; TraceReader&lt;Key&lt;'a&gt; = Tr1::Key&lt;'a&gt;, Time = G::Timestamp, Diff = Diff&gt;\n            + Clone\n            + 'static,\n        for&lt;'a&gt; Tr1::Key&lt;'a&gt;: ToDatumIter,\n        for&lt;'a&gt; Tr1::Val&lt;'a&gt;: ToDatumIter,\n        for&lt;'a&gt; Tr2::Val&lt;'a&gt;: ToDatumIter,\n    {\n        // Reuseable allocation for unpacking.\n        let mut datums = DatumVec::new();\n\n        if closure.could_error() {\n            let (oks, err) = self\n                .linear_join_spec\n                .render(&amp;prev_keyed, &amp;next_input, move |key, old, new| {\n                    let mut row_builder = SharedRow::get();\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n\n                    let mut datums_local = datums.borrow();\n                    datums_local.extend(key.to_datum_iter());\n                    datums_local.extend(old.to_datum_iter());\n                    datums_local.extend(new.to_datum_iter());\n\n                    closure\n                        .apply(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage, &amp;mut row_builder)\n                        .map(|row| row.cloned())\n                        .map_err(DataflowError::from)\n                        .transpose()\n                })\n                .inner\n                .ok_err(|(x, t, d)| {\n                    // TODO(mcsherry): consider &#96;ok_err()&#96; for &#96;Collection&#96;.\n                    match x {\n                        Ok(x) =&gt; Ok((x, t, d)),\n                        Err(x) =&gt; Err((x, t, d)),\n                    }\n                });\n\n            (oks.as_collection(), Some(err.as_collection()))\n        } else {\n            let oks =\n                self.linear_join_spec",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "(",
            "lineNumber": 467
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;self,",
            "lineNumber": 468
          },
          {
            "text": "        prev_keyed: Arranged&lt;S, Tr1&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 469
          },
          {
            "text": "        next_input: Arranged&lt;S, Tr2&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 470
          },
          {

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "        // to scan for these potential errors. Note that we cannot directly use\n        // &#96;mz_timely_util::reduce::ReduceExt::reduce_pair&#96; here because we only\n        // conditionally render the second component of the reduction pair.\n        if let Some(mfp) = mfp_after2 {\n            let mfp_errs = arranged\n                .mz_reduce_abelian::&lt;_, RowErrBuilder&lt;_, _&gt;, RowErrSpine&lt;_, _&gt;&gt;(\n                    \"ReduceMonotonic Error Check\",\n                    move |key, input, output| {\n                        let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                        let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                        let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                        datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n                        let accum = &amp;input[0].1;\n                        for monoid in accum.iter() {\n                            datums_local.extend(monoid.finalize().iter());\n                        }\n                        if let Result::Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage)\n                        {\n                            output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                        }\n                    },\n                )\n                .as_collection(|_k, v| v.clone());\n            (output, validation_errs.concat(&amp;mfp_errs))\n        } else {\n            (output, validation_errs)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the dataflow to compute and arrange multiple accumulable aggregations.\n    ///\n    /// The incoming values are moved to the update's \"difference\" field, at which point\n    /// they can be accumulated in place. The &#96;count&#96; operator promotes the accumulated\n    /// values to data, at which point a final map applies operator-specific logic to\n    /// yield the final aggregate.\n    fn build_accumulable&lt;S&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        collection: VecCollection&lt;S, (Row, Row), Diff&gt;,",

        "contents": "                        }\n                        match (&amp;aggr.func, &amp;accum) {\n                            (AggregateFunc::SumUInt16, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. })\n                            | (AggregateFunc::SumUInt32, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. })\n                            | (AggregateFunc::SumUInt64, Accum::SimpleNumber { accum, .. }) =&gt; {\n                                if accum.is_negative() {\n                                    error_logger.log(\n                                    \"Invalid negative unsigned aggregation in ReduceAccumulable\",\n                                    &amp;format!(\"aggr={aggr:?}, accum={accum:?}\"),\n                                );\n                                    let key = key.to_row();\n                                    let message = format!(\n                                        \"Invalid data in source, saw negative accumulation with \\\n                                         unsigned type for key {key}\"\n                                    );\n                                    output.push((EvalError::Internal(message.into()).into(), Diff::ONE));\n                                }\n                            }\n                            _ =&gt; (), // no more errors to check for at this point!\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    // If &#96;mfp_after&#96; can error, then evaluate it here.\n                    let Some(mfp) = &amp;mfp_after2 else { return };\n                    let temp_storage = RowArena::new();\n                    let datum_iter = key.to_datum_iter();\n                    let mut datums_local = datums2.borrow();\n                    datums_local.extend(datum_iter);\n                    for (aggr, accum) in full_aggrs2.iter().zip_eq(accums) {\n                        datums_local.push(finalize_accum(&amp;aggr.func, accum, total));\n                    }\n\n                    if let Result::Err(e) = mfp.evaluate_inner(&amp;mut datums_local, &amp;temp_storage) {\n                        output.push((e.into(), Diff::ONE));\n                    }\n                },\n            );",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/compute/src/render/context.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 562
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 607,
            "column": 22
          }
        },
        "contents": "    /// input. Only the first &#96;max_demand&#96; columns are decoded. Pass &#96;usize::MAX&#96; to\n    /// decode all columns.\n    pub fn flat_map&lt;D, I, L&gt;(\n        &amp;self,\n        key_val: Option&lt;(Vec&lt;MirScalarExpr&gt;, Option&lt;Row&gt;)&gt;,\n        max_demand: usize,\n        mut logic: L,\n    ) -&gt; (Stream&lt;S, I::Item&gt;, VecCollection&lt;S, DataflowError, Diff&gt;)\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator&lt;Item = (D, S::Timestamp, Diff)&gt;,\n        D: Data,\n        L: for&lt;'a&gt; FnMut(&amp;'a mut DatumVecBorrow&lt;'_&gt;, S::Timestamp, Diff) -&gt; I + 'static,\n    {\n        // If &#96;key_val&#96; is set, we should have to use the corresponding arrangement.\n        // If there isn't one, that implies an error in the contract between\n        // key-production and available arrangements.\n        if let Some((key, val)) = key_val {\n            self.arrangement(&amp;key)\n                .expect(\"Should have ensured during planning that this arrangement exists.\")\n                .flat_map(val.as_ref(), max_demand, logic)\n        } else {\n            use timely::dataflow::operators::Map;\n            let (oks, errs) = self\n                .collection\n                .clone()\n                .expect(\"Invariant violated: CollectionBundle contains no collection.\");\n            let mut datums = DatumVec::new();\n            let oks = oks.inner.flat_map(move |(v, t, d)| {\n                logic(&amp;mut datums.borrow_with_limit(&amp;v, max_demand), t, d)\n            });\n            (oks, errs)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Factored out common logic for using literal keys in general traces.\n    ///\n    /// This logic is sufficiently interesting that we want to write it only\n    /// once, and thereby avoid any skew in the two uses of the logic.\n    ///\n    /// The function presents the contents of the trace as &#96;(key, value, time, delta)&#96; tuples,\n    /// where key and value are potentially specialized, but convertible into rows.\n    fn flat_map_core&lt;Tr, D, I, L&gt;(\n        trace: &amp;Arranged&lt;S, Tr&gt;,\n        key: Option&lt;&amp;Tr::KeyOwn&gt;,\n        mut logic: L,\n        refuel: usize,",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    /// input. Only the first &#96;max_demand&#96; columns are decoded. Pass &#96;usize::MAX&#96; to",
            "lineNumber": 563
          },
          {
            "text": "    /// decode all columns.",
            "lineNumber": 564
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub fn flat_map&lt;D, I, L&gt;(",
            "lineNumber": 565
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;self,",

          {
            "text": "        mut logic: L,",

          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; (Stream&lt;S, I::Item&gt;, VecCollection&lt;S, DataflowError, Diff&gt;)",
```

---


Here’s how the error is supposed to flow and where it can get lost.

---

## Where the error is produced

1. **`RangeBound::canonicalize()`** (range.rs) returns `Err(InvalidRangeError::UnsupportedRangeElementType(...))`.
2. **`CastStringToRange::eval`** (string.rs) does `range.canonicalize()?`, so that becomes `Err(EvalError::InvalidRange(e))` via `From<InvalidRangeError>`.
3. So the error **is** returned from scalar evaluation as `Err(EvalError::InvalidRange(...))`.

---

## Two main execution paths

**Path A – Scalar kept in the plan (your `SELECT ... FROM tfloat` case)**  
- Input is `Get(tfloat)`, not a constant, so the plan stays as something like `Map { input: Get, scalars: [ binary(@>, cast_string_to_range(...), column(0)) ] }`.
- At **runtime**, for each row we eval that scalar:
  - `CallBinary::eval` does `a = a_expr.eval(...)?` (the LHS is the cast).
  - That runs `CastStringToRange::eval` → `canonicalize()` → `Err(...)`.
  - The `?` returns `Err(EvalError::InvalidRange(...))` from the binary’s `eval`.
- In compute’s map stage, that `Err` is passed to the **error stream** (e.g. `err_session.give((e.into(), time, diff))`), and the render layer builds the “errs” tree from it. So the error **should** show up as a normal evaluation error to the user.

**Path B – Constant folding**  
- If the input were constant, `fold_constants` might evaluate the same scalar and get `Err(e)`.
- It doesn’t use `?` to fail the transform; it stores the error in the plan: `Constant { rows: Err(e), typ }`.
- When that plan is **rendered**, `render.rs` does:
  - `Err(e) => (Vec::new(), vec![e])`
  - and sends that `e` into the error collection.
- So the error still surfaces at execution time via the err stream.

So in both paths the design is: the error propagates as `EvalError` and is emitted on the **error** side of the dataflow (oks vs errs), and the adapter should present that to the user.

---

## Why it might look like it’s “not propagating”

1. **Optimization vs execution**  
   If the failure happened in the **old** code (before your fix), it was a **panic** in `canonicalize()`. That panic is caught by `catch_unwind_optimize` and turned into `TransformError::Internal("unexpected panic during query optimization: ...")`. So the user saw an *optimizer* error, not an evaluation error.  
   With your fix, there is no panic; we return `Err`. So:
   - If the scalar is **never evaluated** during optimization (e.g. no constant folding of that expression), the plan is built successfully and the `Err` will only occur when the map is **executed**.
   - So you must **run** the `SELECT` (not just prepare/explain) to see the new error.

2. **Where the error appears**  
   Eval errors are often delivered on the **error stream** of the dataflow (separate from the successful rows). The adapter may show that as a distinct error result or message. So the error might be propagating but shown in a different place (e.g. “error in stream” vs “main query error”).

3. **No `From<EvalError>` for `TransformError`**  
   In transforms like `fold_constants`, when we do `scalar.eval(...)?`, the closure’s error type is `EvalError`. The transform returns `Result<(), TransformError>`. There is no `From<EvalError> for TransformError`, so that `?` would not compile there. In the code you have, the inner closure is typed as `Ok::<_, EvalError>(...)`, and the **outer** code doesn’t `?` that inner `Result`; it stores `Err(e)` in `Constant { rows: Err(e) }`. So in that path the error is not “propagated up the stack” as a `TransformError`; it’s embedded in the plan and only surfaces when the constant is rendered (path B above). That’s by design.

---

## What to do

- **Run the query** (e.g. `SELECT (('[1.0,100.0)')::floatrange @> tfloat.c0) IS TRUE FROM tfloat`) and check the **evaluation error** (and the error stream), not the optimizer message.
- If you want the failure to happen **during planning** when the expression can be fully constant-folded, you’d need to use a path that simplifies to a literal and then turns that into a `PlanError`. That’s what `simplify_to_literal_with_result()` in `hir.rs` does: it returns `PlanError::ConstantExpressionSimplificationFailed` when the simplified expression is `Literal(Err(err), _)`. So if the **whole** expression (e.g. for a LIMIT clause) is constant and reduces to that error literal, that’s where you’d see a plan-time error. For a non-constant expression like your `SELECT ... FROM tfloat`, the error is only produced at execution time and should appear as an eval error.
- Adding an sqllogictest that runs this query and expects the “range type over Float64 is not supported” (or similar) message will confirm that the error is propagated and visible to the user.

---

_**User**_

So the issue is, I'm just seeing this:

```
materialize=> CREATE  TABLE  t5(c0 UINT2);                                                                                            
CREATE TABLE                                                                                                                          
materialize=> SELECT (('[1,100)')@>(t5.c0)) IS TRUE  FROM t5;                                                                         
 ?column?                                                                                                                             
----------                                                                                                                            
(0 rows)        
```

I was expecting to see an error because before I was seeing panics. But I'm just getting ?column?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_


The behavior comes from **when** the scalar is evaluated.

Your query is:

```sql
SELECT (('[1,100)')@>(t5.c0)) IS TRUE FROM t5;
```

- The plan is something like: **Map** over **Get(t5)** with one scalar: `@>(cast_string_to_range('[1,100)'), column(0))`.
- Scalar evaluation runs **per row**. So the `CastStringToRange::eval` (and thus `canonicalize()`) path is only run when there is at least one row to process.

If **t5 is empty** (0 rows), the Map never runs `eval` for any row, so:

- `canonicalize()` is never called.
- No `InvalidRangeError::UnsupportedRangeElementType` is ever produced.
- You just get an empty result: `?column?` and `(0 rows)`.

So the error **does** propagate when evaluation actually happens; with an empty table it never runs.

To see the error:

- Insert a row, then run the same SELECT, e.g.:

  ```sql
  INSERT INTO t5 VALUES (1);
  SELECT (('[1,100)')@>(t5.c0)) IS TRUE FROM t5;
  ```

  You should then get the “range type over UInt16 is not supported” (or similar) error when the engine evaluates the expression for that row.

If you want the error even when the table is empty (e.g. “range type over uint2 is not supported” at plan time), that has to be enforced earlier:

- In the SQL/planning layer: when choosing the `@>` overload or when allowing `String -> Range(...)`, reject unsupported range element types (e.g. UInt16, Float32, Float64) and return a **plan error** instead of building a plan that would only fail at execution. That’s the Option A (reject with clean error) we discussed: add a check in `func.rs` and/or `typeconv.rs` so these types are rejected before any evaluation.

---

_**User**_

but before, with the unreachable, it was panicking even with an empty table?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_


Yes. The difference is **where** the failure happens.

**Before (with `unreachable!`):**  
During **optimization**, `reduce()` tries to constant-fold expressions. It does something like:

```text
eval = |e| MirScalarExpr::literal(e.eval(&[], temp_storage), ...)
```

So it **evaluates** the cast with empty datums (e.g. `CastStringToRange` on `'[1,100)'`), which calls `canonicalize()`. That hit the `unreachable!()` and **panicked inside the optimizer**. The panic was caught by `catch_unwind_optimize` and turned into “unexpected panic during query optimization”. So you saw a failure even with an empty table, because the failure was during planning/optimization, not during per-row execution.

**After (returning `Err`):**  
The same constant-fold still runs, but now `canonicalize()` returns `Err(...)` instead of panicking. So `e.eval(&[], temp_storage)` gives `Err(InvalidRangeError::UnsupportedRangeElementType(...))`, and we build:

```text
Literal(Err(...), typ)
```

No panic, so optimization **succeeds** and the plan is stored with that error literal in it. At **runtime**, the error would only be produced when we **evaluate** that scalar for a row. With an empty table, the Map never runs for any row, so we never evaluate that literal and the error never appears; you just get 0 rows.

So:

- **Before:** fail during optimization (panic in constant-fold) → error even with 0 rows.  
- **After:** no panic; error is only possible when we eval a row → with 0 rows you see no error.

To get a **user-visible error even with an empty table**, you have to fail earlier than “first row eval”:

1. **During optimization:** when we produce a `Literal(Err(e), _)` (e.g. in `reduce()` or a follow-up pass), treat that as a failed optimization and surface `e` (e.g. convert to `TransformError` and then to a plan/optimizer error). That would restore “fail when we would have panicked before,” even for 0 rows.
2. **During planning:** reject unsupported range element types when building the plan (e.g. in `func.rs` / `typeconv.rs`) so we never build a plan that needs `Range(UInt16)` or similar. Then you get a clear plan error (e.g. “range type over uint2 is not supported”) even with an empty table.

So the reason you no longer see an error with an empty table is that we moved from “panic during constant-fold” to “error only when evaluating a row,” and with 0 rows we never evaluate. Fixing that means either failing when we create that error literal during optimization, or rejecting the type at plan time.

```sql
INSERT INTO t5 VALUES (1);

```

Then you should get the “range type over UInt16 is not supported” error (or similar) instead of 0 rows.

```json
        },
        "contents": "    Ok(Plan::Select(plan))\n}\n\nfn plan_select_inner(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    select: SelectStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    params: &amp;Params,\n    copy_to: Option&lt;CopyFormat&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(SelectPlan, RelationDesc), PlanError&gt; {\n    let when = query::plan_as_of(scx, select.as_of.clone())?;\n    let lifetime = QueryLifetime::OneShot;\n    let query::PlannedRootQuery {\n        mut expr,\n        desc,\n        finishing,\n        scope: _,\n    } = query::plan_root_query(scx, select.query.clone(), lifetime)?;\n    expr.bind_parameters(scx, lifetime, params)?;\n\n    // OFFSET clauses in &#96;expr&#96; should become constants with the above binding of parameters.\n    // Let's check this and simplify them to literals.\n    expr.try_visit_mut_pre(&amp;mut |expr| {\n        if let HirRelationExpr::TopK { offset, .. } = expr {\n            let offset_value = offset_into_value(offset.take())?;\n            *offset = HirScalarExpr::literal(Datum::Int64(offset_value), SqlScalarType::Int64);\n        }\n        Ok::&lt;(), PlanError&gt;(())\n    })?;\n    // (We don't need to simplify LIMIT clauses in &#96;expr&#96;, because we can handle non-constant\n    // expressions there. If they happen to be simplifiable to literals, then the optimizer will do\n    // so later.)\n\n    // We need to concretize the &#96;limit&#96; and &#96;offset&#96; of the RowSetFinishing, so that we go from\n    // &#96;RowSetFinishing&lt;HirScalarExpr, HirScalarExpr&gt;&#96; to &#96;RowSetFinishing&#96;.\n    // This involves binding parameters and evaluating each expression to a number.\n    // (This should be possible even for &#96;limit&#96; here, because we are at the top level of a SELECT,\n    // so this &#96;limit&#96; has to be a constant.)\n    let limit = match finishing.limit {\n        None =&gt; None,",

        "contents": "fn plan_expr_inner&lt;'a&gt;(\n    ecx: &amp;'a ExprContext,\n    e: &amp;Expr&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;CoercibleScalarExpr, PlanError&gt; {\n    if let Some((i, item)) = ecx.scope.resolve_expr(e) {\n        // We've already calculated this expression.\n        return Ok(HirScalarExpr::named_column(\n            i,\n            ecx.qcx.name_manager.borrow_mut().intern_scope_item(item),\n        )\n        .into());\n    }\n\n    match e {\n        // Names.\n        Expr::Identifier(names) | Expr::QualifiedWildcard(names) =&gt; {\n            Ok(plan_identifier(ecx, names)?.into())\n        }\n\n        // Literals.\n        Expr::Value(val) =&gt; plan_literal(val),\n        Expr::Parameter(n) =&gt; plan_parameter(ecx, *n),\n        Expr::Array(exprs) =&gt; plan_array(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::List(exprs) =&gt; plan_list(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::Map(exprs) =&gt; plan_map(ecx, exprs, None),\n        Expr::Row { exprs } =&gt; plan_row(ecx, exprs),\n\n        // Generalized functions, operators, and casts.\n        Expr::Op { op, expr1, expr2 } =&gt; {\n            Ok(plan_op(ecx, normalize::op(op)?, expr1, expr2.as_deref())?.into())\n        }\n        Expr::Cast { expr, data_type } =&gt; plan_cast(ecx, expr, data_type),\n        Expr::Function(func) =&gt; Ok(plan_function(ecx, func)?.into()),\n\n        // Special functions and operators.\n        Expr::Not { expr } =&gt; plan_not(ecx, expr),\n        Expr::And { left, right } =&gt; plan_and(ecx, left, right),\n        Expr::Or { left, right } =&gt; plan_or(ecx, left, right),\n        Expr::IsExpr {\n            expr,\n            construct,\n            negated,\n        } =&gt; Ok(plan_is_expr(ecx, expr, construct, *negated)?.into()),\n        Expr::Case {\n            operand,\n            conditions,\n            results,\n            else_result,\n        } =&gt; Ok(plan_case(ecx, operand, conditions, results, else_result)?.into()),\n        Expr::HomogenizingFunction { function, exprs } =&gt; {\n            plan_homogenizing_function(ecx, function, exprs)\n        }\n        Expr::NullIf { l_expr, r_expr } =&gt; Ok(plan_case(\n            ecx,\n            &amp;None,",
```
